name: sidecar
description: Claude Code plugin pack with strict concept separation. Top-level dirs: hooks/ (PreToolUseВ·SessionStart auto-behavior), commands/ (/slash-command invoked), skills/ (Skill tool invocable). 1 plugin = 1 concept; no mixing.

plugins.name: commons
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook вҖ” injects a cross-project do/dont layer from commons.tape (granular `@D g0..g77` form, no tool/usage) + a live `pool` roster snapshot (~/.pool/pool.json) + a live cwd `./pods.json` per-project pod/job manifest snapshot appended per injection so the host lineup AND the /system SSOT are visible without running `pool list` / `hexa cloud pods` first; UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the layer never fades; SessionStart additionally appends COMMANDS.md (the cross-project slash-command catalog) once per session. Carries LATTICE_POLICY.md (real-limits-first SSOT). 0.20.0 (2026-05-30) sharpens @D g47 (PR ship discipline) with a learned operational rule вҖ” run `gh pr merge` / `--delete-branch` from the CANONICAL repo dir, NOT a linked worktree's cwd (a worktree holds 'main', so merging from inside it errors `main is already used by worktree`); commons @V 1.8вҶ’1.9. The git-guard `git worktree remove --force` false-positive is filed as a separate source-fix handoff (g11/g59), not worked around. 0.19.0 (2026-05-30) adds @D g77 (credential exists-check before register / login вҖ” run `secret list` + check known aliases huggingface.tokenвҶ”hf.token before claiming a credential missing; the 2026-05-30 HF-token incident), shipped in lockstep with its enforcement вҖ” the new `secret-guard` 0.1.0 PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook (s7 вҖ” rule + enforcement together). 0.18.0 (2026-05-29) renders M5 pod project attribution in `_pods_snapshot()` вҖ” each rented pod shows `<id>[<provider>,<status>] @<project> В· "<purpose>"` (empty/absent project вҶ’ `@вҡ unattributed`, purpose omitted when empty), so a SessionStart reader sees WHICH project owns each billing pod (matches the hexa-lang `pod_registry.hexa` project/purpose fields + the cross-project `cloud down` guard вҖ” anima RTSC near-miss fix). 0.16.0 (2026-05-29) sharpens @D g8 into an explicit deny+redirect вҖ” ALL direct runpod/vast provider CLI (any verb incl. read/lifecycle) + raw ssh/scp/curl + REST/GraphQL API + python/node SDK forbidden, all access routes through `hexa cloud` full pod lifecycle (rentВ·downВ·listВ·execВ·runВ·nohupВ·copyВ·preflight); commons @V 1.5вҶ’1.6 В· lockstep with cloud-guard 0.3.0 enforcement (s7). 0.13.0 (2026-05-29 В· CLOUD M10) repoints `_pods_snapshot()` to the GLOBAL M5 registry `~/.hx/cloud/active-pods.json` (cwd-independent В· cost-bearing В· `<id>[<provider>,<status>]` + `$<cost>`) + adds `_providers_snapshot()` (M8 catalog `~/.hx/cloud/providers.json` one-liner). Both global вҶ’ always visible В· absent вҶ’ silent. 0.12.0 (2026-05-28) added `_pods_snapshot()` on cwd `./pods.json` (superseded by M5 global registry); pair feature with `pods-route` hook (auto `--register`). 0.11.0 (2026-05-28) drops the `enabled` per-host field + `[os,on|off]` rendering from the roster snapshot вҖ” roster hosts are always-on (pool 0.8.9 ships the matching always-on model on the CLI side); the SessionStart roster line now renders `name[os]` and pi5-akida exclusion is handled downstream by pool-route's name-substring akida match. 0.10.15 adds @D g75 (candidate orchestration вҖ” build phase vs fire phase split, domain-agnostic вҖ” pairs with micro-exp 0.4.0 Stage 1.5 + cycle 0.9.2 decision tree).

plugins.name: prefs

plugins.name: git-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) + SessionStart git-staleness safety guard, in hexa-lang (`_git_guard.hexa` + `_git_guard_session.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Five layers, all no-network (FETCH_HEAD mtime is the freshness proxy вҖ” the hook NEVER runs `git fetch`). DOWNSTREAM: (1) DENIES force-type push вҖ” `git push --force` / `-f` В· `--force-with-lease` В· `git push <remote> +<refspec>` (refspec-level force); quotes stripped before tokenizing (0.4.2) so `'--force-with-lease=x'` / `+"refspec"` can't slip past. (2) STALE-BASE PUSH advisory (0.5.0, non-blocking): for a non-force push, emits additionalContext when the current branch trails the remote default base (origin/HEAD, else origin/main) by >= 20 commits вҖ” the shared-workdir branch-swap / silent-revert fingerprint; push still proceeds. (3) STALE-BASE MERGE advisory (0.6.0, anima #1105, non-blocking): a `gh pr merge` (squash) reaches main via a path the push-time check never saw вҖ” it revert-deleted 35190 files in #1105 вҖ” so when the command contains `gh pr merge` (no `git push`, not cross-repo `--repo X`), runs the same divergence probe and warns before the merge lands (real deny on mass deletion lives in pr-cycle-hook's deletion-sanity gate). UPSTREAM (0.7.0): (4) CREATION advisory вҖ” on `git worktree add` / `git checkout -b` / `git switch -c`, warns when the base does not resolve to `origin/<default>` (local main/HEAD/omitted) OR `.git/FETCH_HEAD` is older than 30 min; steers to `git fetch origin` then base off `origin/<default>`. (5) SessionStart advisory вҖ” `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/<default>...<default>` against already-fetched refs; if local default is >= 1 behind origin it nudges `git fetch origin && git pull --ff-only` (the shared-tree drift where a sibling agent merged to origin), plus a fetch nudge when FETCH_HEAD is stale; silent when up-to-date / not a repo / no origin. Hook-bypass (--no-verify) NOT blocked (left to user discipline). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: workdir-guard
plugins.description: SessionStart advisory (hexa-lang `_workdir_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`) that fires once per session, and only when the current git working tree is shared by >= 2 concurrent `claude` agents. Detection (~0.1s): `ps` lists claude PIDs (comm == 'claude' вҖ” lsof's command field shows the version string, not 'claude'), then one `lsof -a -p <list> -d cwd` collects their cwds, awk prefix-matched against `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. A shared working tree = shared `.git/index` + `HEAD`, so a sibling agent can swap HEAD out from under you, commingle your staged files, or leave your push on a stale branch. Injects a one-shot reminder to do git landings in an isolated worktree and never reset/switch/delete another agent's active branch. Grouped by working tree, not repo вҖ” silent in solo sessions (count 1) and self-extinguishes once you move into a worktree. Advisory-only, non-blocking. Proactive complement to git-guard's reactive stale-base push advisory. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: worktree-guard
plugins.description:
PreToolUse(Bash) advisory for `git worktree add`, in hexa-lang (`_worktree_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`). When a Bash command creates a git worktree, emits non-blocking systemMessage with the durable-worktree drill: in a heavily-concurrent or synced repo an isolated worktree dir can be removed out from under you (a sibling agent's `git worktree prune`, a workspace sync, or the macOS /tmp reaper) and any UNCOMMITTED edits vanish with it вҖ” so commit + push promptly after editing, don't hold uncommitted work across turns, land via PR then `git worktree remove` (verification builds AFTER the commit, so a failed build never costs the work). Stronger note when the target path is under a volatile location (/tmp В· /private/tmp В· /var/folders). BRANCH-REUSE advisory (0.2.0, anima #1105): when `git worktree add -b
` reuses an existing branch вҖ” especially a LOCAL-only stale one absent from origin (a rejected/unpushed prior turn's leftover) вҖ” warns of the stale-base risk that revert-deleted 35190 files in #1105 and suggests a fresh per-PR branch (`
-$(date +%s)` from origin/main). Advisory-only (guards-narrow-scope: non-blocking systemMessage > deny for hygiene). Sister of workdir-guard (shared-tree SessionStart advisory) + worktree-gc (merged-worktree prune). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: memory-lint
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory for the auto-memory index file (`memory/MEMORY.md`), implemented in hexa-lang (`_memory_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Reads the post-write file when tool_input.file_path ends in `memory/MEMORY.md` and emits non-blocking systemMessage for: entry count > 30 (hot-tier soft cap вҶ’ migrate older items to sibling MEMORY.archive.md) В· any non-comment line > 150 chars (TLDR refactor budget вҖ” verb + nouns, push trailing clauses into the sibling `*.md` file). Keeps the always-loaded MEMORY.md dense + greppable as memories accumulate. Advisory-only by design (per guards-narrow-scope: non-blocking systemMessage > deny for hygiene).

plugins.name: plan-guard
plugins.description: Enforces step-by-step plan locked-decision adherence вҖ” the marker + hook + linter triad closing the three observed plan-drift root causes (orchestrator writes an escape into the sub-agent prompt that contradicts a locked decision В· mid-design drift off the original plan В· sbs Auto-QA conformance is a soft self-check that misses it). CORE (0.1.0): the `plan-lint` engine (`_plan_lint.hexa`, via `hexa run`) parses a plan.md's `## locked decisions` `@L<n> вҖҰ assert:<kind> <arg>` machine-readable contract (step-by-step 0.8.0 marker) and verifies each assert against the repo or, with `--diff <filesвҖҰ>`, a restricted file set вҖ” `grep <pat>` (term present В· `!pat`=absent) В· `file <path>` (exists) В· `verdict <slug>/<id>` (`.verdicts/<slug>/<id>.txt` non-empty). Per-`@L` вң“/вң— verdict + NONZERO exit on any unmet; a plan with no `@L` lines is skipped (exit 0, backward-compatible). Advisory by design вҖ” the bundled hooks only emit additionalContext, never deny. Mirrors the memory-lint hexa-lint pattern (defensive readers В· JSON-escaped additionalContext В· no network). 0.2.0 adds the PreToolUse(Task|Agent) inject hook (`_plan_inject.hexa`): on every sub-agent spawn it resolves the ACTIVE plan (frontmatter `status: active`, else newest-mtime drafts/*-plan.md), extracts the `@L` locked-decision lines and injects them VERBATIM as additionalContext so the orchestrator can't silently write a contradicting escape into the spawn prompt (root cause #1 вҖ” the '.kosmos н•„мҲҳ' вҶ’ 'SKIP OK' incident), plus an escape scan (skip/ignore/мғқлһө/вҖҰ near a locked term or human-label token) that appends an advisory warning on a possible contradiction. Always exits 0 (advisory). 0.3.0 adds the PostToolUse(Bash) ship-lint hook (`_plan_ship.hexa`): fires after a ship command (`git commit` / `gh pr вҖҰ`), runs the PR-2 plan-lint engine against the shipped diff (`git diff origin/main...HEAD` file set, else staged/HEAD, else repo-wide) and surfaces any UNMET `@L` asserts as a verbatim additionalContext warning so a mid-design drift off the locked plan (root cause #2, e.g. 'AKIDA-first вҶ’ GPU') is caught at ship where sbs Auto-QA's soft conformance self-check (root cause #3) missed it. Non-blocking вҖ” always exits 0. 0.3.1 hardens active-plan resolution (@L6): the newest-mtime fallback now SKIPS plans explicitly closed (`status: done`/`abandoned`), so a flipped-done plan's `@L` is never enforced against a later unrelated session вҖ” paired with the step-by-step 0.8.1 closure flip. 0.3.2 documents the dogfood self-test (build plan's @L1~@L6 all PASS against landed artifacts; 2 honest findings logged).

plugins.name: subagent-resilience
plugins.description: рҹӘӮ PreToolUse(Task|Agent) hook that UNCONDITIONALLY injects a backoff+resume survival contract into every sub-agent dispatch via additionalContext вҖ” the mechanical enforcement of the cross-session resilience rule so future dispatches can't forget it. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_resilience_inject.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). On every Task/Agent spawn it emits a 4-clause verbatim contract: (1) BACKOFF вҖ” on rate-limit / 429 / 'overloaded' / throttle errors, do NOT abort, wait + retry with exponential backoff before treating as fatal; (2) CHECKPOINT вҖ” commit work in small logical units AS YOU GO (commit-early) so progress is durable, push when a unit completes; (3) RESUME вҖ” if restarted / re-dispatched, do NOT start over, inspect the last pushed commit and continue; (4) REPORT-ON-HALT вҖ” if you must stop before completion, report the last-good checkpoint (commit sha + what remains) so the parent can re-dispatch. The ONLY gate is `tool_name вҲҲ {Task, Agent}` вҖ” unlike plan-guard there is NO plan-file / marker / env-var / flag / config gating, injected on EVERY dispatch (commons @D s11 вҖ” no escape hatch; @D s7 вҖ” the hook IS the enforcement, no prose rule). Contract text is INLINE hardcoded (immutable constant), reads stdin via `exec("cat")` (hexa native, avoids fake-FILE segfault), portable `$HOME`/`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths only. additionalContext only вҖ” NO updatedInput, NO permissionDecision вҖ” so it stacks safely alongside plan-guard + subagent-route at the same PreToolUse(Task|Agent) surface. Always exits 0. Mirrors the plan-guard inject-hook pattern. Known limit: additionalContext is advisory prompt steering, not a transport-layer retry вҖ” it shapes sub-agent behavior, it does not patch the API client.

plugins.name: drift-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory that prevents design-drift regression вҖ” when a design-defining surface is edited but MEMORY.md + CLAUDE.md/project.tape are not, the next session (which loads memory at startup, not the diff) regresses to the old design. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_drift_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Fires on any of: (1) SSOT file edit вҖ” path ends in `.tape` or `/CLAUDE.md`; (2) a new artifact under a `decisions/` folder; (3) an explicit `@design-change` marker in the post-write content. Skips the mirror targets themselves (`/memory/`, MEMORY.md) and the guard's own install dir. Emits non-blocking systemMessage nudging the model to mirror the change into the project auto-memory (memory file + MEMORY.md pointer), or вҖ” for a cross-project governance rule вҖ” to ask the user to `sidecar sign` commons.tape/project.tape. De-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. Advisory-only. 0.1.1 вҖ” kills self-trigger noise: sentinel scan now limited to source-code extensions (.hexa/.py/.sh/вҖҰ), so prose-heavy docs (marketplace/README/CHANGELOG/SKILL) that DOCUMENT the marker no longer self-trigger on every edit; sidecar repo's own /hooks/drift-guard/ tree is now skipped (in addition to CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT cache). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: verdict-gate
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit) hard block + Stop advisory enforcing commons @D g73 (a verdict is EARNED by independent recompute, never self-judged вҖ” anti-fake-closure). Strict filename gate: fires ONLY when the target basename matches `H_*.md` or equals `result.json`. Layer (A) HARD DENY вҖ” a Write/Edit that asserts a verdict token (рҹ”ө / рҹҹў / SUPPORTED-FORMAL / SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL) in such a file, while the backing repo-root `.verdicts/<slug>/` (slug = `H_<slug>.md` stripped of `H_`+`.md`, or the containing dir name for result.json) holds no non-empty `*.txt` (verbatim `hexa verify` stdout), is denied with a redirect to run `hexa verify` and persist the output first. Repo root = nearest ancestor with a `.git`, else cwd. Layer (B) ADVISORY (non-blocking systemMessage) вҖ” a `.hexa` write containing a tautological smoke (a `check`'d `fn` whose single-statement body returns the literal/param it is compared against) warns 'tautological smoke вҖ” not verification (g73)'. Layer (C) Stop ADVISORY вҖ” if recent (mtime < 6h) `H_*.md` files assert рҹ”ө/рҹҹў with 0% `.verdicts/` backing, emits a non-blocking Stop reason 'loop self-judging only В· STOP & report (g73)'; never a hard stop-block (a hard block could trap an autonomous loop). Non-applicable tokens вҡӘ SPECULATION-FENCED / рҹҹЎ CITATION / рҹҹ  INSUFFICIENT NEVER fire (already honest). Plain `.md` prose mentioning рҹ”ө (the RFC, commons.tape, README rubric) is NOT caught вҖ” the filename gate excludes it. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_verdict_gate.hexa` + `_verdict_stop.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Closes the standing commons @D s7 gap (g73 landed without its enforcement hook). NO opt-out by design (commons @D s11 вҖ” gated only on real preconditions: missing payload skip, filename gate, token absent skip, evidence present skip).

plugins.name: sidecar-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) auto-lint that fires on `git commit` in any Claude Code marketplace plugin pack (any repo with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json), implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Non-blocking findings as additionalContext: stale-history patterns in staged diff (commons @D g15) В· hardcoded /Users//home/ absolute paths in staged diff (commons @D g13 В· sidecar @D s3) В· marketplace.json plugin entry вҶ” each plugin.json version drift (commons @D g22) В· hook .sh missing user-exec bit В· missing CHANGELOG.md at repo root (commons @D g29) В· (when .claude-plugin/profiles.json is present) any marketplace plugin missing a tier there (sidecar @D s7 вҖ” untagged silently defaults to 'personal', hidden from the minimal/hexa profiles) В· any registered STDIO MCP-server plugin (project.tape @D s15 вҖ” `mcps/` source or command-based `mcpServers`; STDIO MCP forbidden, its stdin/stdout JSON-RPC handshake collides with hexa hook stdio + fake-stdin; an HTTP/SSE-transport `mcpServers` with a `url` is PERMITTED, not flagged). 0.10.0 вҖ” added a SKILL.md s6 check (project.tape @D s6): each `skills/*/SKILL.md` body (after frontmatter) is flagged when it holds >1 `@D` block, prose/markdown-heading/table/code-fence beyond do/dont, a @D key other than do/dont, or when a per-skill `README.md` exists вҖ” closes the s7 enforcement gap (s6 was required-active with no lint). 0.9.0 вҖ” s15 relaxed to allow HTTP/SSE MCP (stdio still flagged). 0.8.1 вҖ” the top-level-dir check (project.tape @D s16) is an ALLOWLIST not a name-blocklist: any tracked top-level dir that is NOT a concept dir (`hooks`В·`commands`В·`skills`В·`agents`)/`bin`/`.github`/`.claude-plugin` is flagged, so renaming a bucket (`docs`вҶ’`documentation`) does NOT dodge it; the advisory directs co-location under the owning domain/plugin. Tracked-only so gitignored runtime/scratch never false-fires. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: hexa-native
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block for `.py` / `.sh` / `.c` / `.cc` / `.cpp` / `.cxx` / `.h` / `.hpp` / `.hh` / `.inl` / `.ipp` / `.tcc` / `.s` / `.S` / `.asm` / `.o` writes inside any project rooted at a directory containing a `project.tape` marker (sidecar's canonical project identity file), implemented in hexa-lang (`_hexa_native.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.8.0 adds C++ inline/template headers `.inl` / `.ipp` / `.tcc` + preprocessed-asm `.S` (uppercase) + `.asm` (0.7.0 `.hpp` / `.hh`; 0.6.0 `.cpp` / `.cxx` / `.h`; 0.5.0 `.cc` / `.s` / `.o`; 0.4.0 `.c`; on top of `.py` / `.sh`) вҖ” a hexa-native project takes `.hexa` only; the full hand-written C / C++ / header / inline / asm / object family is refused (regenerated via `hexa cc --regen`, never hand-edited per commons @D g61); each suffix matches literally + case-sensitively (`.S`вү `.s`, `.h`вү `.sh`/`.hexa`, `.o`вү `.go`/`.io`). Covers BOTH write channels: structured file tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit) and the Bash write channels вҖ” `> X.inl`, `>> X.S`, heredoc-to-redirect (`cat > X.py << EOF`), `tee [-a] X`, plus non-redirect channels `dd of=X` and `cp`/`mv` destination (0.3.2); relative targets resolved against the payload `cwd`. Quote-aware redirects; fd-dup (`2>&1`) yields no target; non-write Bash (`python X.py`, `cat X.c`, `gcc -c X.c -o X.o`, `chmod X.sh`) passes through (compiler `-o` output is NOT a covered channel). Unbounded channels (`python -c open(...)`, `sed -i`, `perl -e`, `install -t`) are NOT covered вҖ” this narrows the common write paths, not a sandbox. Walks up parent dirs of the target; if a `project.tape` is found, the write is denied with an action-first message that leads with the fix (re-issue as `.hexa`) and demotes the refusal reason. NO opt-out: no env var, no config file, no exception list, no self-exclusion вҖ” hard-coded by design. Targets only `.py` / `.sh` / `.c` / `.cc` / `.cpp` / `.cxx` / `.h` / `.hpp` / `.hh` / `.inl` / `.ipp` / `.tcc` / `.s` / `.S` / `.asm` / `.o`; other extensions pass through. Projects without `project.tape` are unaffected.

plugins.name: sign-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) sign-gate for governance-SSOT files, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sign_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). A small registry (GATED) of high-stakes files вҖ” currently `commons.tape` (cross-project do/dont layer), `project.tape` (per-project identity + governance), and `.gitignore` (commit-hygiene: silently controls what enters version control, so a stealth agent edit could un-ignore a secret or hide a file from review), and `~/.sidecar/local-paths` (0.1.7 вҖ” the LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST: a registered path-prefix silently overrides pool-route to force commands LOCAL, so a stealth add routes arbitrary commands off the pool) вҖ” may be edited by the agent ONLY while a fresh user sign-off token exists. The user mints one with `sidecar sign <key>` (`commons` В· `project` В· `gitignore` В· `paths`), writing the current epoch to `~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign`; a token is valid for 300s (5 min). Covers both write channels (mirrors hexa-native): structured file tools and the Bash write channels (`> X`, `>> X`, heredoc, `tee X`, `dd of=X`, `cp`/`mv` dest; relative targets resolved against `cwd`). Writes buried inside an invoked CLI (e.g. `sidecar init`) are not visible at the top-level command string, so the official scaffolder is unaffected вҖ” only the agent's own direct writes gate. The hook CODE-ENFORCES the no-self-mint rule: it hard-denies the agent's mint command `sidecar sign <key>` (list / clear stay allowed) AND any write forging a token under `~/.sidecar/signs/`. Because the agent's tool calls (Bash В· Write/Edit) route through this hook, the agent CANNOT self-mint. Mint detection (0.1.5) is narrowed to a real command invocation: the Bash channel is scanned on a quote-masked copy and sidecar must sit at a command position (first token or right after a shell separator), so a sidecar sign <key> MENTION inside a quoted commit message or PR body is no longer mis-flagged - pre-0.1.5 it wrongly blocked legit commits that referenced the command. (The interactive `!` bang a USER types in the TUI prompt does NOT route through the hook and mints freely вҖ” the intended user path, safe because the agent cannot emit a `!` bang.) `bin/sidecar` carries an OPT-IN /dev/tty second layer (`SIDECAR_SIGN_REQUIRE_TTY=1`) defeating hook-parser-evading obfuscation. The fresh token is the sole precondition (project.tape @D s11: gate on a real precondition, not an opt-out switch); enforcement is the hard deny (project.tape @D s13). To gate another file: add one row to GATED and reship. NO env-var / flag override by design.

plugins.name: plist-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit) hard block for `.plist` writes, implemented in hexa-lang (`_plist_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Any target file ending in `.plist` (LaunchAgents В· LaunchDaemons В· Info.plist В· UserDefaults) is denied with a redirecting message вҖ” the agent cannot distinguish user-explicit from model-autonomous intent, so all .plist writes are refused globally; if the user truly wants one, they run the command themselves outside the agent path. LaunchAgents / LaunchDaemons are persistence surfaces (auto-launch on login / boot), so silent writes risk installing daemons the user did not ask for. Enforces commons @D g37 (plist user-request only). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: cloud-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for DIRECT rented-GPU provider CLI / API usage вҖ” ALL runpod / vast.ai access routes through `hexa cloud` (commons @D g8), which now wraps the FULL pod lifecycle (rent В· down В· list/status В· exec В· run В· nohup В· poll В· tail В· copy-to/from В· preflight). Five orthogonal detectors run on every Bash command: (a) pod-CLI + verb pair scan anywhere вҖ” a provider CLI (`runpodctl` В· `vastai` В· `vast`) followed by ANY recognized verb is denied: read (list/get/show/search/status), lifecycle (create/start/stop/remove/destroy/launch/config/project/version), AND remote-exec/transfer (exec/ssh/send/receive/scp/copy/port/attach-ssh) вҖ” 0.3.0 closes the prior read+lifecycle pass-through (`runpodctl pod list` leaked to the pool); the following-verb requirement keeps `which runpodctl` / `man vastai` (no verb) passing; (b) ssh/scp/rsync/sftp against `*.runpod.io` / `proxy.runpod.net` / `*.vast.ai` hosts; (c) curl/wget/http(ie)/xh against `api.runpod.io` / `api.runpod.ai` / `rest.runpod.io` / `runpod.io/graphql` / `console.vast.ai` / `api.vast.ai` / `vast.ai/api` URLs; (d) dispatcher-script content scan вҖ” `bash/sh/zsh/dash <path>` reads the script and re-runs detectors (a)/(b)/(c) on its body; (e) inline-runtime provider-SDK probe вҖ” `python(3)?`/`node`/`deno`/`bun` with `-c`/`-e` importing the runpod / vastai SDK (raw API use); committed SDK code passes through (mirrors ai-api-guard); (f) M5 pod-registry write-guard (0.4.0) вҖ” any Bash command targeting `~/.hx/cloud/active-pods.json` with a mutating op (`>`/`>>`/tee/sed/cp/mv/dd/truncate/rm/install/ln) is denied (reads cat/jq/grep/head/tail/wc/less pass; `hexa cloud вҖҰ` passes вҖ” it builds the path internally), paired with a Write|Edit|MultiEdit matcher denying any file-write tool whose `file_path` basename is `active-pods.json` вҶ’ ALL registry writes route through `hexa cloud {rent|adopt <id> --project P --purpose T|down <id>|watchdog}` (atomic temp+rename inside hexa, invisible to PreToolUse вҶ’ 0 FP on hexa's own writes). Shell separators normalized to whitespace before tokenizing вҖ” `cd /x && runpodctl ssh вҖҰ`, `bash -c 'вҖҰ vast exec вҖҰ'`, env-prefixes all caught. Redirects to `hexa cloud {rent|down|list|run|nohup|poll|copy-to|copy-from|preflight}` with per-verb mapping. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_cloud_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: secret-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) ADVISORY hook enforcing commons @D g77 (credential exists-check before register / login), in hexa-lang (`_secret_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Seals the recurring 'credential is missing / please register' anti-pattern when the key ACTUALLY EXISTS in the `secret` store (the 2026-05-30 HF token incident: an agent saw a `huggingface.token` 401 and told the user to register, but `hf.token` held a valid token). Two detectors consult `secret list` (key NAMES only вҖ” values NEVER read/printed): (a) `secret set <K>` where <K> already exists вҶ’ advises read via `secret get <K>`, overwrite only if rotating; (b) credential-acquisition commands `hf auth login` / `huggingface-cli login` / `gh auth login` / `aws configure` / `<tool> login` when a plausibly-corresponding key EXISTS (serviceвҶ’key heuristic: hf/huggingface вҶ’ `hf`/`hugg`; gh/github вҶ’ `github`/`gh`; aws вҶ’ `aws`), surfacing the matching key names + a known-alias hint (huggingface.token вҶ” hf.token). ADVISORY ONLY вҖ” `permissionDecision:"allow"` + `additionalContext`, NEVER deny (commons @D g5 / guards-narrow-scope: must not block legitimate re-registration / rotation); mirrors git-guard's non-blocking emit. CONSERVATIVE вҖ” fires only on a real match; `secret list` / `secret get` / `--help` / bare reads pass SILENTLY. Shell separators normalized before tokenizing. FAILS OPEN on any error. NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11).

plugins.name: deck-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block for hand-authoring a `.in` input deck вҖ” forces the `/deck` generator (canonical `stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` SSOT). ALL writes whose target basename ends in `.in` / `.in.head` / `.in.tail` are denied + redirected to `/deck`, EXCEPT a WHITELIST of build-system templates: Makefile.in / makefile.in / GNUmakefile.in / configure.in / config*.in (autoconf incl. config.h.in) В· *.pc.in В· *.spec.in В· Doxyfile.in В· *.pro.in, PLUS (Write/Edit) content carrying an autoconf `@VAR@` substitution marker (generic autoconf template, not a QE/DFT deck). Two channels (mirrors hexa-native): structured tools (file_path) + Bash (`>`/`>>` В· heredoc В· tee В· dd of= В· cp/mv dest; Bash uses basename whitelist, @VAR@ content-whitelist applies to Write/Edit). hexa-lang (`_deck_guard.hexa`). The whitelist is a SCOPE precondition (build templates are not decks), not a disable switch вҖ” NO opt-out by design (@D s11).

plugins.name: pod-monitor
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for GPU pod fires (`hexa cloud nohup` / `hexa cloud fire` / `hexa cloud run`). Emits non-blocking systemMessage reminding the agent of commons @D g57 вҖ” four invariants: set `SAVE_POD=1` envar (pod retain when SSH drops), attach the рҹӣ°пёҸ Monitor tool to the streaming log (no `tail -f` / sleep-poll), вҸұ estimate walltime up front with pod-hours headroom (undersize вҶ’ pod auto-killed mid-run вҶ’ result LOST), AND рҹ“Ӣ register the fired pod in cwd's `./pods.json` via `hexa cloud dispatch add <jid> <pid> <dir>` (cloud@0.4.0 + hexa-lang PR #1699 В· ghost-pod class вҖ” caller fires but never registers вҶ’ per-project ledger / billing / verdict lost). Closes the R8a-LOST class + the ghost-pod class. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_pod_monitor.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.1.4 вҖ” pods.json ledger hint added (caller-side responsibility; hexa cloud fire/nohup does NOT auto-attach upstream-side). 0.1.3 вҖ” `_has_pod_fire` uses token-position match past env-var prefix (the cloud verb must SIT at command position, not be a substring of the cmd string) so commit messages / grep patterns / docs that merely mention the phrase no longer trigger noise. `fire` verb (hexa-lang PR #1309) added. 0.1.5 вҖ” dead retired-rule source citation in the hexa replaced with the current no-opt-out rule (project.tape @D s11). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: monitor-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for background / long-running shell launches (trailing `&` В· `nohup`/`setsid`/`disown` В· the Bash tool's `run_in_background` flag). When the rate-limit-survivable pattern is incomplete, emits non-blocking systemMessage enforcing commons @D g10 вҖ” the while-monitor pattern: (1) detach the job (`nohup`/`setsid`) so a session rate-limit death does NOT SIGHUP it, (2) write a progress log (`> /tmp/<job>.log 2>&1`) as a durable record + Monitor re-attach point, (3) attach the рҹӣ°пёҸ Monitor tool to the LOG (not the live pipe вҖ” token-light, re-attachable after a rate-limit), with an OS-side `while` heartbeat for liveness (no model-side sleep-poll). Stays silent when the job is already detached AND logged. Closes the rate-limit-force-kill class for general local background work; general-local sister of pod-monitor (GPU pods) and proactive sister of limit-guard (post-hoc checkpoint). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_monitor_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.1.1 вҖ” `hexa cloud nohup`/`hexa cloud fire` patterns ALWAYS fire a cloud-bridge hint (basic detach+log is inherent to the verb, but the cloud workflow has a separate requirement: thread the logfile path from nohup into `hexa cloud tail` so Monitor can attach). 0.1.2 вҖ” the `hexa cloud fire` verb + `__MONITOR_HANDLE__={вҖҰ}` JSON-line contract are now SHIPPED upstream (hexa-lang PR #1306 + #1309); cloud-bridge hint points to the live atomic workflow (`hexa cloud fire <host> -- <argv>` вҶ’ grep handle line вҶ’ attach Monitor to `tail_cmd`) instead of the pre-ship 3-step manual handoff. 0.1.3 вҖ” cloud-bridge trigger now uses token-position match past env-var prefix (`_is_cloud_pod`) instead of `cmd.contains(...)` substring вҖ” kills the noise where commit messages / grep patterns merely mentioning the phrase fired the advisory. Also fires the hint even when `is_bg` would short-circuit (cloud fire/nohup are inherently dispatch verbs). 0.1.4 вҖ” `has_detach` follows the same token-position pattern via `_has_detach_pos`. 0.1.5 вҖ” `has_log` also via `_has_log_pos` + `_redirect_targets` (quote-aware extraction of `>`/`>>` redirect targets, `tee` invocations with file args, `<<` heredocs) вҖ” commands that merely MENTION `.log` in an unrelated arg (e.g. `grep nohup /var/log/foo.log`) no longer satisfy the log-sink check incorrectly. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: throttle-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Task|Agent) cross-session coordinator for the TRANSIENT server throttle вҖ” 'Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) В· Rate limited' вҖ” that fires when too many concurrent requests hit at once (several sessions, or a wide fan-out). Distinct from limit-guard (the 5h/7d USAGE/session cap вҶ’ checkpoint + RESUME). On detect: (1) writes/extends a SHARED cooldown marker ~/.sidecar/throttle.json with an exponential backoff (15В·30В·60В·120В·240В·300s, capped, 120s storm window) so every session/round reads ONE backoff window вҖ” killing the thundering-herd re-fire where all sessions retry in lockstep and re-trip the throttle; (2) injects a non-blocking directive вҖ” back off with JITTER (never retry in lockstep), cut fan-out WIDTH to вүӨ1 until the window passes, resume by re-reading not re-running. Concurrency control that RESPECTS the throttle (clears once in-flight load drops), not evasion. Gated to NOT double-fire with limit-guard (skips when the result names a usage/session limit). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_throttle_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: ai-api-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw AI-API calls when a sidecar CLI wraps the same operation, implemented in hexa-lang (`_ai_api_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Two deny patterns: (1) `curl`/`wget`/`http`/`httpie` to an AI hostname (`api.openai.com` В· `api.anthropic.com` В· `fal.run` В· `api.replicate.com` В· `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`); (2) inline `python(3)?` / `node` / `deno` / `bun` with `-c`/`-e` AND `import` of `openai` / `anthropic` / `fal_client` / `replicate` / `google.generativeai`. Redirects to `/imagine` (image) / `/research` (text) / etc. вҖ” commons @D g50 enforcement. Whitespace/line-continuation is normalized before matching (0.1.3) so multi-space / split forms still match; dynamic imports (`exec(...)`, base64) remain out of scope (static scan limit). General SDK usage in committed code files (no inline `-c`/`-e`) passes through. 0.2.0 вҖ” s14 capacity-relief detector added: a non-blocking advisory (additionalContext, NOT a deny) fires when a Bash command or Write/Edit content SETS/EXPORTS/WRITES an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (or wires `--api-key` for anthropic) as a Claude rate-limit/capacity workaround, redirecting to the sanctioned CLI routes (account swap via `/quota` В· CLI routers В· Bedrock/Vertex backends) per project.tape @D s14; matcher widened to Bash|Write|Edit. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: verify-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw verification-tool usage cited as primary evidence, implemented in hexa-lang (`_verify_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Denies first-token вҲҲ {wolframscript, mathematica, WolframKernel}, and inline-Python verification via `python(3)? -c '...'` containing `import sympy` / `from sympy` / `import pyphi` / `import wolframclient` with a redirecting message pointing to `hexa verify` (commons @D g5). General sympy / wolframclient usage in committed code files passes through вҖ” only inline `-c` verification probes (and the standalone Wolfram CLIs) are refused. 0.1.3 вҖ” dead retired-rule source citation in plugin.json replaced with the current no-opt-out rule (project.tape @D s11). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pr-cycle
plugins.description: /pr-cycle вҖ” one-shot PR cycle command. Pushes the current branch (`git push -u origin HEAD`) then `gh pr create --fill`; the `pr-cycle-hook` plugin's PreToolUse hook then auto-appends ` && gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch` (+ worktree/branch cleanup when run from a linked worktree) вҖ” one command does create вҶ’ merge вҶ’ clean. Refuses on main/master. Pass extra gh flags as args. Command half of the pr-cycle split (routing hook = `pr-cycle-hook`).

plugins.name: pr-cycle-hook
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) PR full-cycle router for `gh pr create`, in hexa-lang (`_pr_cycle.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Quote-aware tokenizer scans for the `gh pr create` 3-gram anywhere вҖ” catches bare and chained (`git push && gh pr create вҖҰ`) forms. When matched (not draft / not already merging), rewrites via the `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput вҖ” REQUIRED or the rewrite is ignored) to append the merge tail and, IF cwd is a linked worktree, ` && (cd <main-wt> && git worktree remove <this-wt> --force; git branch -D <branch>)` вҖ” atomic create вҶ’ merge вҶ’ worktree+branch cleanup. Cross-repo `--repo X` routes the merge to X, skips local cleanup. 0.3.0 вҖ” explicit PR# capture via `gh pr view --json number -q .number` (cross-repo via `gh pr view --repo X`); robust when branch-ref is detached / worktree mid-teardown / CI strips head-ref; cross-repo pins both view+merge to the target repo. DELETION-SANITY GATE (0.2.0, anima #1105): before appending the auto-merge, probes the squash payload (`git diff --name-status <merge-base(origin/main,HEAD)>...HEAD`, or `gh pr diff` cross-repo) for a mass-deletion outlier вҖ” D-count > 50, OR deletions >= 10x additions and > 20 abs вҖ” and DENIES the merge (PR stays created, the dangerous squash-merge is withheld) to block stale-base regressions like the 35190-file revert-delete. Fail-open on probe failure. Enforces commons @D g47. Skip: `--draft` В· already contains `gh pr merge`. Hook half of the pr-cycle split. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: tape-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Edit|Write) deny for `.tape` edits, implemented in hexa-lang (`_tape_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run` вҖ” no Python, no shell shim). Four checks, all diff-aware (pre-existing violations grandfathered, only newly-introduced or worsened items block). (1) fields rule вҖ” `@D` blocks accept only `do` / `dont`; new `why` В· `tool` В· `note` В· `ref` В· `ex` В· ... refused. Fires on any `*.tape` path. (2) length cap вҖ” `do` / `dont` value > 100 chars refused (split overflow). commons.tape + project.tape only. (3) authoring-language вҖ” when `sidecar prefs` `code` axis is `english`, lines newly introducing non-Latin scripts (Hangul В· CJK В· Hiragana В· Katakana) are refused. commons.tape + project.tape only. (4) @I siblings вҖ” project.tape's @I block must carry a `siblings` field; dropping it or adding a fresh @I without it is refused (cross-repo network SSOT). project.tape only. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pool-route
plugins.description: 0.17.0 FIND HOST-INTROSPECTION: `find` joins the host-introspection LOCAL-EXECUTION whitelist (alongside du/df/ps/lsof/вҖҰ) вҖ” it walks the LOCAL filesystem tree, so routing it to a pool host returns the PEER's tree (wrong for a disk survey / file search on this Mac). The 0.9.0 launch deliberately excluded `find` to spare the workstation a heavy `find /` scan, but that conflated 'heavy' with 'portable': a local-fs-bound command must stay local regardless of CPU cost (@D s10 вҖ” route on portability, never on load). First-token basename match; no opt-out (@D s11). 0.16.0 DECK-GEN PIN BROADENED: the deck local-pin match widened from the single absolute path-string `stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` to IDENTITY-based вҖ” also catches a relative `deck/gen.hexa` form (run from inside the stdlib dir / a symlinked cwd) AND a future `hexa deck` verb form, so NO deck-generator invocation path can slip to a pool resource (bypass the local pin); the cloud pin already matches `hexa cloud` adjacency so it needs no broadening. 0.15.0 DECK-GEN LOCAL PIN (s3 companion): a 6th structural LOCAL-EXECUTION exemption pins any command referencing `stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` to LOCAL вҖ” the `/deck` runnable became `$HOME/.hx/install/hexa-lang/stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` (s3 portability) so it no longer carries a `/Users/` literal; without the pin it would route to a Linux pool host where that Mac-local hx artifact is absent and break. Mirrors the cloud/atlas pins; unconditional, no opt-out (@D s11). 0.14.0 SIDECAR CLI LOCAL-BOUND (routing bug fix): the bare `sidecar <verb>` CLI joins the local-bound early-exit whitelist alongside `git`/`gh`/`pool` (`_has_word(cmd, "sidecar")`). The `sidecar` marketplace CLI is host-LOCAL вҖ” it manages `~/.sidecar/` (handoff registry В· signs В· local-paths whitelist В· profiles) + `~/.claude/` (installed_plugins.json В· enabledPlugins) вҖ” so `sidecar handoff add вҖҰ` / `sidecar sync` mis-routed to a pool host (ubu-2) wrote the WRONG machine's state (or 127'd where no `sidecar` binary exists). The bare CLI was NOT covered pre-fix: the $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA literal pin (0.6.8) + the `~/.sidecar` dotstate pin (0.6.9) only catch commands literally carrying those tokens, and `sidecar handoff add <repo> <text>` carries neither. Mirrors the `pool` pin exactly (sidecar-family local CLI В· @D s10 local-bound cmds stay local). 0.13.0 MINI-EQUAL TOGGLE DROPPED (user feature 2026-05-28): the `~/.pool/mini-equal` marker is no longer read вҖ” general-heavy round-robin is permanently Linux-only (Mac stays the workstation per @D s12). macOS pool hosts (mini) still receive macOS-only auto-dispatches (swift / xcodebuild / codesign / вҖҰ); explicit `pool on mini <cmd>` is the manual escape for ad-hoc macOS work. Companion ship: pool 0.8.10 (drops `pool mini-equal` + `pool clean on|off` verbs + the `clean` per-host field). Together with 0.12.0 this retires the last on/off-style toggles in the pool stack. 0.12.0 ALWAYS-ON ROSTER (user feature 2026-05-28): the per-host `enabled` field is no longer read вҖ” every roster host listed in `~/.pool/pool.json` is treated as on for routing-candidate purposes. Drops the residual `enabled=true` filter at the head of the eligibility scan; the existing pi5-akida name-substring exclusion (anima-only В· @D s8) is the ONLY remaining gate, so behavior is identical for the current roster (mini В· ubu-1 В· ubu-2 routing-eligible, pi5-akida excluded) but the model and `pool list` are no longer cluttered with an on/off toggle that never actually toggled. Companion ships: pool 0.8.9 (drops `shared:false` field + `[ded]` marker В· akida exclusion via the same name-substring helper) + sidecar commons 0.11.0 (SessionStart roster snapshot renders `name[os]` instead of `name[os,on|off]`). 0.11.0 FORK-STORM CAP (user feature 2026-05-28): a whitelist-forced-LOCAL hexa run escapes the default hexaвҶ’pool offload and runs on the Mac; when FORK_STORM_CAP (default 8) or more USER hexa interpreters are already live locally, a NEW whitelist-local hexa is DENIED with a runbook вҖ” locality preserved (imports still resolve), storm capped. Counts via `ps -Ao args` (first-token basename `hexa`, one line per run), excludes sidecar's own hooks (`cache/sidecar`) + `claude` (@D s9), fails OPEN on a count error so it never blocks (@D s11). 0.10.0 SIDECAR PATH-PREFIX WHITELIST (user feature вҖ” chat-form sbs FULL agreement 2026-05-28): a new permanent LOCAL-EXECUTION exemption layer reads `~/.sidecar/local-paths` (line-based plain text, USER-managed via the new `sidecar paths {bare|add|rm}` CLI verb) вҖ” any Bash cmd whose argv contains a `/`-prefixed absolute-path token that starts with a registered prefix is FORCED LOCAL (skip pool routing). PERMANENT counterpart to the runtime `sign-local` token (which is one-shot 30-min); no TTL. The hook scans ALL argv tokens, tolerates a missing file (empty whitelist В· no fail-closed В· no caching since the file is typically 2-10 lines), and skips blank + `#` comment lines. Seeded on first use by `sidecar paths` with sidecar's own cache + repo paths (`~/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/` + `~/core/sidecar/`) so sidecar's own hexa hooks/commands stop being routed to the pool вҖ” closes the recurring fail mode where the cache (workstation-only) was sent to a pool peer. Sits between host-introspection and the heavy classifier in the routing decision; emits a one-line `pool-route: local (sidecar path whitelist В· prefix=<matched>)` on hit. The whitelist itself IS the opt-in вҖ” no env opt-out (project.tape @D s11). Companion CLI: `sidecar paths` lists, `sidecar paths add` registers cwd, `sidecar paths add <dir>` registers an abs-resolved dir (refuses non-existent / already-present), `sidecar paths rm <prefix>` removes a line (refuses if not found). 0.9.3 DATA-LOCALITY PIN + SIGN-LOCAL TTL 30min (anima PURE F-CURRICULA-1): a local-data-dependent `hexa run` (Mac-local corpus input) was blocked on BOTH routing paths. (a) data-locality pin (5th structural LOCAL-EXECUTION exemption): a hexa exec whose data-INPUT flag (--corpus-path/--corpus/--data/--data-path/--dataset/--input/--train-data/--eval-data/--weights/--ckpt/--checkpoint) points at a LOCAL-ONLY path (anything NOT under a synced ~/core/ workdir) now PINS LOCAL вҖ” the pool peer has no copy of that data, so routing reproduced the observed failure (`hexa run ... --corpus-path /Users/.../c.jsonl` to ubu-2, compile-stage `source not found` = a data-absence error misread as a code error). Synced ~/core data still routes (peer mirrors it). Same data-locality necessity as the host-introspection whitelist; quote-stripped tokens; unconditional, no opt-out env (s11). (b) sign-local TTL raised 5min to 30min (LOCAL_SIGN_TTL=1800 in _local_signed()): a single legitimate local build (corpus regen + compile) runs 10-20min, exceeding the old 5min `! sidecar sign local` window, so a mid-build `hexa run` re-tripped the fork-storm sign gate on token expiry (re-sign every 5min was unworkable; F-CURRICULA-1 fire BLOCKED twice). TRADE-OFF: the fork-storm guard window is now 6x longer, accepted (the local token is explicit, user-minted with no agent self-mint, single-key, clearable early via `sidecar sign clear local`); a build-covering window is the Occam fix (g0) vs a sliding-window/session-marker mechanism. The local token TTL is INDEPENDENT of the governance sign TTL (SIGN_TTL=300 in bin/sidecar + hooks/sign-guard, gating agent EDITS to commons/project/gitignore, stays 5min); bin/sidecar mint message + --list are now key-aware (sign_ttl_for: local=1800, governance=300). 0.9.1 ROLLBACK of 0.8.3 canon: each pool host's hexa binary has host-specific path-resolution (ubu-1 = bash wrapper exec'ing hexa_real; ubu-2 = ELF binary with argv[0]-relative `self/native/hexat` search В· symlinks were Mac-paths broken on Linux). HEXA_REAL_BIN/HEXA_LANG=canon injection works on some hosts, silently breaks on others (qwen_bpe.hexa `run` needs `self/native/hexat` at a host-specific path canon doesn't supply consistently). 0.9.1 reverts to host-owns-its-hexa: pool-route uses host's `~/core/hexa-lang` for `HEXA_LANG` and host's PATH-resolved `hexa` binary. Stale-stdlib is the host owner's responsibility (anima for ~/core/hexa-lang drift on ubu-2). `~/.hx/canon` + canon-update.sh remain as scaffolding on hosts where they were bootstrapped; pool-route no longer routes through canon. POLICY INVERSION (0.9.0 вҖ” мң м Җ directive "л¬ҙмЎ°кұҙ pool В· нҷ”мқҙнҠёлҰ¬мҠӨнҠёл§Ң local к°ҖлҠҘ"): default = POOL. Only LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST hits stay local вҖ” heavy-vs-light gating + default-local fallthrough REMOVED. Whitelist (closed list, sign-gated additions): sidecar self-hooks ($CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/DATA) В· hexa cloud В· hexa atlas register В· git/gh В· /Users//home abs-path heavy-interp (sign-gated) В· ~/. dotstate В· npm/pnpm/yarn В· pool CLI В· HOST-INTROSPECTION (0.9.0 new вҖ” ps В· top В· uptime В· w В· who В· df В· du В· free В· vmstat В· iostat В· uname В· sysctl В· launchctl В· systemctl В· pgrep В· pkill В· kill В· killall В· stat В· lsof В· netstat В· ifconfig В· ip В· arp В· route В· dscl В· scutil В· sw_vers В· first-token basename match В· semantic-necessity not convenience) В· `! sidecar sign local` 5min token. Pre-0.9.0 heavy classifier retained for OS-capability hints only (is_macos/is_linux вҶ’ which pool host). The `find /` root-tree case (anima 100%+ CPU 2:44+) now routes by default. SSH overhead accepted. TERMINOLOGY (0.8.4): 'WHITELIST' = LOCAL-EXECUTION allowlist (things that escape pool dispatch В· stay local) вҖ” the 0.8.0 'HEXA WHITELIST' phrasing was misleading and the rule was renamed to HEXA POOL-DISPATCH. Real whitelist members: $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/DATA В· hexa cloud В· hexa atlas register В· /Users//home abs-path heavy interp (sign-gated) В· ~/. dotstate В· git/gh В· npm/pnpm/yarn В· 5min `! sidecar sign local` runtime token. Registration onto the whitelist is sign-gated by design (commons @D s13 for hardcoded structural exemptions via project.tape В· TUI `! sidecar sign local` for runtime force-local В· no agent self-mint path). No env-var opt-out (commons @D s11). Pool-canonical hexa routing (0.8.3): pool dispatches pin hexa to `~/.hx/canon` (pool-owned origin/main checkout, independent of each host's `~/core/hexa-lang` which may carry feature branches / behind state / anima WIP). Wrapper-injection via `HEXA_REAL_BIN=$HOME/.hx/canon/build/hexa_linux` + `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/.hx/canon` + `HEXA_DAEMON=0`. Bootstrap / refresh via `~/.hx/canon-update.sh` (idempotent: git fetch origin main + reset --hard + copy bootstrap C from ~/core/hexa-lang + hexa cc --regen). Solves the stale-compiler / qwen_bpe miscompile class (ubu-2 was 614 commits behind on stale main + DNS-broken вҶ’ fetch impossible; canon is independent of that drift). Probe: canon binary exists + wrapper executes `hexa --help` through canon. Hosts missing canon fail preflight with a runbook deny-trailer. HEXA WHITELIST (0.8.0 вҖ” POOL-OFFLOAD m8): ANY hexa execution (hexa/hexa.real/hexac/hexadrv/hxv2 as the real first-token after env-prefix+wrapper+backslash unwrap) routes to the pool вҖ” full stop, replacing the heavy-VERB blacklist that leaked verify/parse/atlas/honesty вҶ’ multi-hundred-process Mac fork-storm. Only 4 STRUCTURAL exemptions stay local (handled earlier, never reach the whitelist): $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA hexa (sidecar's own hooks вҖ” routing the router is incoherent), `hexa cloud` (itself a dispatcher), /Users//home/~/. abs-path hexa (sign-gated, path may be absent on peer), and `hexa atlas register` (0.8.1 вҖ” mutates local SSOT embedded.gen.hexa + commits locally; routing breaks the share workflow вҖ” only the register WRITE subverb, read forms still route). The legacy heavy_words/heavy_pairs classifier remains for NON-hexa heavy compute (make/cargo/gcc/node/java/ffmpeg/вҖҰ). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has вүҘ1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the hexa whitelist or the non-hexa heavy classifier (make В· cargo В· go build В· swift build В· docker build В· pytest В· nvidia-smi В· train В· hexa kick/drill/loop/cc/run/build В· aprime_cc В· `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan В· вҖҰ) or a root-needing command, it is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput) to run on a pool host over ssh вҖ” transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Routing is EXPLICIT-classifier only: the load-escalation gate (promote any non-trivial cmd to heavy on high summed non-claude CPU) is absent because on a multi-core Mac that threshold trips constantly and ships local-bound git/hexa-run/smoke commands to Linux hosts where their paths don't exist. Local-bound guard (extended 0.6.9): `git`/`gh` commands, any `/Users/`В·`/home/` absolute-path literal, and any `~/.`В·`$HOME/.` home-dotstate reference always run local вҖ” dotstate forms (`~/.zsh_history`В·`~/.hexa-cache`В·`~/.pool`В·`~/.sidecar`В·вҖҰ) are per-host hidden state, not user-synced, so routing them reads the WRONG host's history/cache; `~/core/...` synced workdirs still route. Sidecar plugin pin (0.6.8): cmds carrying `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` or `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` literals always run local вҖ” they resolve via the dispatching bash to the workstation's plugin cache, which only exists on the local Mac; routing them fails (cache absent on the pool host). Trusted surface (every sidecar slash command writes its dispatch via `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` per @D s3), so no sign gate. Local-bound sign gate: within the `/Users/`В·`/home/` exemption, HEAVY invocations (hexa CLI any verb В· direct `hexa.real` В· python/python3/py В· gcc/g++/clang/clang++/cc/c++ В· `bash`/`sh <script>`) require a fresh `local` sign-off token вҖ” the canonical mac fork-storm trigger; USER mints with `! sidecar sign local` in the TUI prompt. Bare absolute-path reads (ls/cat/grep/find/...) stay free вҖ” the gate narrows to interpreters/compilers only. macOS pool-host enable (0.6.6 вҖ” supersedes 0.6.1 Zero-macOS-offload): macOS pool hosts (e.g. `mini` with `os: macos`) are now auto-dispatch candidates for macOS-only commands (swift build В· xcodebuild В· codesign В· вҖҰ); general-heavy round-robin still excludes macOS hosts so generic build/test fan-out stays off the workstation. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + the command's toolchain actually runnable - `command -v hexa` + `test -x build/hexa_interp` + `hexa <verb> --help` for hexa, `command -v <compiler>` for C/C++, 0.6.13), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown вҖ” never silently falls back to local. `hexa kick`/`drill`/`loop`/`cc` get `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang`; other hexa commands get `HEXA_LANG=$PWD`. PATH + `HEXA_MODULE_LOADER` env passthrough for bare ssh shells. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100); SessionStart reads the last 5 back as additionalContext. Cloud pin (0.6.10): `hexa cloud *` always runs local вҖ” it is ITSELF a Mac-local-only remote-dispatch tool, and its post-`--` remote argv (nvidia-smi В· train.log В· make) tripped the classifier, non-deterministically shipping `hexa cloud exec <pod> -- ...` to a Linux host with no `cloud` subcommand; double-routing a dispatcher is incoherent. WorktreeвҶ’canonical-root fallback (0.6.10): a heavy command in a git LINKED worktree outside $HOME (e.g. /tmp/wt-x) previously DENIED; it now resolves the MAIN checkout via `git worktree list --porcelain` and mirrors that root when it is under $HOME (a synced ~/core workdir). Runs ONLY in the previously-denying branch, so it cannot regress a routing command. C/C++ compiler routing (0.6.11): the C/C++ compiler drivers (gcc / g++ / clang / clang++ / cc / c++) join the heavy classifier - a C/C++ compile is heavy local compute, same workstation-protection rationale as make/cmake/cargo; they carry no macOS-capability marker, so they round-robin across Linux pool hosts only (the Mac stays the workstation, per @D s12). Absolute-path compiler sign gate (0.6.12): the C/C++ compiler drivers also join the local-bound sign gate within the absolute-path exemption - an absolute-path compile (e.g. /usr/bin/gcc on a /Users path) is the same mac fork-storm trigger as a heavy interpreter, so it now needs a fresh local sign-off token (! sidecar sign local) like hexa/python/sh; relative-path compiles in synced workdirs still route to the pool. First-token basename match keeps it precise. Toolchain preflight + akida exclusion (0.6.13): (a) pi5-akida is excluded from the auto-dispatch candidate set entirely (@D s8 - anima-only; reached only via explicit `pool on pi5-akida`). (b) the host preflight now verifies the command's actual toolchain, not just the workdir - every hexa verb probes command -v hexa + test -x ~/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp + hexa <verb> --help, and a C/C++ compile probes command -v <compiler>; a host that fails is SKIPPED so round-robin moves on instead of routing-then-failing (the ubu-1 broken-hexa trap). Known limit (@D d6): a binary that passes --help but segfaults at runtime is not caught. mini-equal toggle (0.7.0): `pool mini-equal on` (writes ~/.pool/mini-equal = "on") opts macOS pool hosts (mini) INTO the OS-agnostic general-heavy round-robin as equal workers; is_linux commands still route Linux-only (the toggle only redistributes portable work). Default OFF preserves zero-macOS-offload (@D s12). Not an in-guard opt-out (@D s11) вҖ” it opens routing to an idle host, does not disable a guard. Single local-execution gate (0.7.2): a fresh `local` sign token (`! sidecar sign local`, 5min) forces EVERY command local вҖ” the one deliberate, user-minted 'run here, do NOT route' path, checked at the top before classification. When the router mis-classifies a local-bound command (diagnostic `ps`/`awk`, a local-fs fix) and ships it to a peer host, the user signs ONCE and routing is suppressed for the window. Subsumes the abs-path-heavy sign gate (signed вҶ’ top-level returns first; unsigned вҶ’ that gate still bites). A sanctioned time-boxed user-minted token, not an env-var opt-out (@D s11). Env-prefix + wrapper hardening (0.7.3): `_local_heavy_interp` now strips POSIX env-var prefix (`POOL_DISABLE=1`/`SIDECAR_POOL_DISABLE=1`/`FOO=bar`/вҖҰ, any IDENT=val вҖ” parser correctness, not specific-var recognition) AND known leading-position wrappers (`env` В· `exec` В· `nice` В· `timeout` В· `command` В· `sudo` В· `nohup` В· `stdbuf` В· `ionice`, with per-wrapper option-arg awareness) AND a leading backslash escape (`\hexa`) before reading the real verb вҖ” pre-0.7.3 any env-var prefix OR any of those wrappers silently ALLOW'd abs-path heavy invocations, breaking sign-local's single-gate integrity. `eval` quoted-string args remain out of scope (require a real shell parser). Node-package installer pin (0.7.4): npm/pnpm/yarn run LOCAL (cwd-dependent, removed from heavy_words + added to local-bound). Routing-rate baseline counters (0.7.5 вҖ” POOL-OFFLOAD m1): every meaningful classifier verdict increments one of 4 counters in ~/.pool/route-counters.tally (routed В· local_bound В· light_allowed В· heavy_failed) so /check reports the % offloaded vs local. Non-classifier exits NOT counted. Race-tolerant. Useless env-prefix advisory (0.7.11 вҖ” agent education): SIDECAR_NO_POOL/POOL_DISABLE env-prefix detection at the head of a Bash cmd emits inline additionalContext advisory explaining no-op + pointing to `! sidecar sign local`. Routing unchanged; purely educational so agent stops re-trying silently. big-tree find generalization (0.7.10 вҖ” POOL-OFFLOAD m5): heavy_find_subs generalized from single `find $HOME/core/anima` to BOTH `find <HOME>/core/` AND `find ~/core/` (tilde form); routes ANY ~/core/* sibling repo find. Preflight test -d still gates each host. Media tools (0.7.9 вҖ” POOL-OFFLOAD m4): heavy_words +2 (ffmpeg/magick) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +4 (ffmpeg/magick/convert/sox В· convert/sox English-word substring-collide вҶ’ first-token-only). JVM compilers + sbt (0.7.8 вҖ” POOL-OFFLOAD m3): heavy_words +3 (javac/kotlinc/scalac) + heavy_pairs +4 (java -jar/sbt compile/sbt test/sbt run В· pair-only for short names) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +5 (java/javac/kotlinc/scalac/sbt). Node ecosystem (0.7.7 вҖ” POOL-OFFLOAD m2): heavy_words +4 (node/deno/bun/ts-node) + abs-path first-token sign-gate adds same +tsx (tsx excluded from heavy_words due to .tsx file-ext collision); mirrors python/hexa fork-storm protection. Heavy hexa verb sweep (0.7.6 вҖ” POOL-OFFLOAD m6): heavy_pairs +19 verbs вҖ” toolchain (hexa test В· hexa bench) В· math verifiers (hexa absolute В· hexa meta-closure) В· math discovery (hexa smash В· hexa free В· hexa hyperarithmetic) В· discovery extras (hexa chain) В· 11 drill variants (omega В· surge В· dream В· swarm В· reign В· molt В· wake В· forge В· canon В· debate В· revive) вҖ” all fork-storm class per the documented hexa 80+ verb catalog. Light verbs (atlas read/lookup В· 29 annotation analyzers В· 16 HTTP data bridges В· lattice verify В· gpu lint/disasm) excluded; 3-token verbs (hexa gpu fire В· sim-universe В· qmirror В· atlas register --from-drill) deferred until richer classifier вҖ” NOTE: superseded by the 0.8.0 hexa whitelist, which routes ALL of these (except `atlas register`, pinned local in 0.8.1). OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance. NO guard opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pods-route
plugins.description: 0.1.0 AUTO-REGISTER PODS (user feature 2026-05-28 В· Layer 2 to commons 0.12.0): PreToolUse(Bash) hook that transparently rewrites `hexa cloud nohup <host> <log> -- <argv>` / `hexa cloud fire <host> -- <argv>` to inject `--register` immediately before the `--` separator when absent, so the launched pod/job auto-lands in cwd `./pods.json` (the per-project /system control-tower SSOT). Idempotent вҖ” commands that already carry `--register` (with or without a `<jid>` argument, including the `--register=<jid>` form) pass through untouched. Closes the canonical AGENT BLINDSPOT: pods.json was cwd-local with no per-turn injection surface, so the agent forgot the SSOT existed and skipped `--register` on launches вҶ’ pods un-tracked вҶ’ `/system` watch/harvest/auto-redispatch silently incomplete + ghost pods (billing leak). Pairs with commons 0.12.0 `_pods_snapshot()` (Layer 1 вҖ” every turn the agent SEES the manifest as a one-line summary or absent-hint). plugins.name: limit-guard

plugins.name: lab
plugins.description: /lab вҖ” research-lab control-tower (domain-agnostic) with a code-level decision harness (0.7.0) + all-paths autonomy (0.8.0). One persistent mission-control over EVERY in-flight job of a long-running campaign across ANY execution surface (rented pods via cloud В· pool hosts В· local procs). Ties the manifest (cwd ./pods.json) + event-driven watchers + harvest+verdict + the autonomous harvestвҶ’re-dispatch loop into one command. NOT domain-locked вҖ” DFT sweeps В· ML training fleets В· build matrices В· render farms В· any N-parallel-job campaign with a queued backlog. Verbs: bare/status В· progress (1.1.0 read-only research-ARC bar) В· tick [--apply] (0.7.0 deterministic probeвҶ’classifyвҶ’decide harness via bin/system_harness.hexa) В· watch В· harvest [<id>] В· next/redispatch В· auto В· drive В· pursue (0.8.0 вҖ” ALL-PATHS autonomy: enumerate EVERY open campaign thread (stalled-resumable jobs В· tooling gaps В· queued waves В· cost-leaks В· un-harvested terminals) + fan ALL out in parallel вүӨ2-3 agents, NO human 'which next?' menu; only genuine human-input gates wait) В· mirror [<seed>] (1.2.0 кұ°мҡёл°© вҖ” DELEGATE to the hexa-loop skill --adapter=atlas, d4 reuse) В· stop В· cost В· queue [add|rm|ls] В· upstream [<repo>]. 1.3.0 ADDS an explicit ALL-PARALLEL dispatch principle (вҶ” demiurge project.tape d_parallel_fire): campaign dispatch fires N candidates across DEDICATED PARALLEL pods (one jobвҶ’one slot В· ranksвүӨphyscores/pod В· OMP/MKL/OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1), NEVER bin-packed into a single-pod sequential onstart.sh chain; a stuck single-pod sequential chain (a flagship blocking the rest) is SPLIT onto parallel pods on sight. Threaded into next/auto/pursue/drive + the queue taxonomy (a `queued` candidate fires in parallel on its own slot; a single-pod sequential chain is the anti-pattern to split). Live lesson: a 6-perovskite batch chained on one vast pod вҶ’ 270-thread/load-119 oversubscription thrash + CaAuH3 blocked 6 other perovskites for days. 1.2.0 ADDS `lab mirror` (the mirror room вҖ” pure delegation to the hexa-loop skill's --adapter=atlas preset, the old mirror-loop miningвҶ’kickвҶ’atlas self-evolution ouroboros, NO reimplementation) + documents a planned lab-lifecycle subcommand set (notebook = append-only journal/decision log В· bench = micro-experiment sweep wrapping /micro-exp В· review = verdict-matrix audit) as stubs. 1.1.0 ADDED `lab progress` вҖ” a read-only campaign-ARC 10-cell bar aggregating verdicts-closed (exports/*/ledger.json + .verdicts/), papers-shipped (PAPER.tape), atlas atom-growth (hexa atlas stats), domain milestones (DOMAINS.tape); each source degrades gracefully (absent вҶ’ dropped from the mean, not 0%). DISTINCT from `status` (per-job operational live dashboard) вҖ” `progress` is the epistemic research-program arc. 1.0.0 RENAMED the skill `system вҶ’ lab` (research-lab metaphor); the `/system` invocation + every prior trigger (кҙҖм ңнғ‘ В· mission control В· control tower В· campaign status) stay live as DEPRECATED aliases вҖ” lossless. All prior verbs preserved verbatim; bin/system_harness.hexa unchanged. Abstract job model. Reuses ./pods.json (cloud dispatch SSOT). Triggers: /lab В· лһ© В· м—°кө¬мӢӨ В· research lab В· lab progress В· м—°кө¬ м§„мІҷлҸ„ В· lab mirror В· кұ°мҡёл°© В· /system (deprecated) В· кҙҖм ңнғ‘ (deprecated) В· мә нҺҳмқё нҳ„нҷ© В· mission control (deprecated) В· campaign status (deprecated) В· нӣ„ліҙ мһҗмңЁ В· лӘЁл“  кІҪлЎң м§„н–ү В· pursue В· tick В· кІ°кіјліҙкі  м¶”к°Җл°ңмӮ¬ В· мһҗмңЁ мһ¬л°ңмӮ¬ лЈЁн”„ В· control tower (deprecated).
