plugins.name: draft
plugins.description: Ephemeral scratchpad scaffolder вҖ” `/draft <slug>` creates `drafts/<slug>.md` for temporary working notes designed to be discarded. The `drafts/` dir is auto-added to .gitignore so files never commit by accident. Verbs вҖ” bare/`list` = enumerate (mtime-sorted) В· `<slug>` = scaffold В· `add <slug> <content...>` = append a timestamped bullet (auto-scaffolds if missing) В· `rm <slug>` = delete one (no confirm) В· `clean` = list `rm` candidates (read-only). LLM AUTO-REGISTRATION (0.2.0) вҖ” SKILL.md instructs the calling LLM to recognize natural-language signals ("мқҙкұ° л“ұлЎқн•ҙмӨҳ"/"л©”лӘЁ"/"м Ғм–ҙл‘¬"/"register this"/"jot down") and proactively run `/draft add <slug> <distilled content>` without an explicit command, picking a slug by first checking existing drafts (reuse if related) else deriving kebab-case from the subject. Symmetric deletion triggers ("мӮӯм ң"/"м§ҖмӣҢ"/"delete that note") map to `/draft rm <slug>`. `/draft` files are explicitly throwaway вҖ” for cross-repo handoffs that should persist, use `sidecar handoff`. Slug accepts alnum / dash / underscore / dot only.

plugins.name: matrix
plugins.description: Axis cross-product coverage tracker вҖ” `/matrix` manages a combinatorial coverage matrix (axis Г— axis hypothesis/experiment grid) via a cwd-local `MATRIX.tape` SSOT, the same declarative pattern as DOMAINS.tape. Two modes: SQUARE (one axis set via `axes <aвҖҰ>`; cells = upper-triangle + diagonal pairs, total NВ·(N+1)/2) and RECTANGULAR (separate `rows <aвҖҰ>` Г— `cols <aвҖҰ>`, e.g. BIO mechanisms Г— anima axes, total RВ·C). Verbs вҖ” bare/`show` renders the full grid with вң“/В· when small (rowsвүӨ14 вҲ§ colsвүӨ14, or square NвүӨ12) else per-row coverage bars в–“в–“в–“в–‘в–‘, always printing coverage % + next 5 unfilled cells В· `done <i> <j>` marks a cell (explicit toggle like /domain milestones; square pairs auto-sorted to a canonical key so order doesn't matter) В· `undone <i> <j>` В· `next [N]` lists next N unfilled (default 10) В· `help`. Drives a systematic combinatorial sweep row-by-row without tracking coverage in prose; SSOT survives across sessions; cells are explicit (not auto-detected) so it's workflow-agnostic. Triggers: /matrix В· л§ӨнҠёлҰӯмҠӨ В· matrix л§Ңл“Өм–ҙ В· көҗм°Ёкіұ л§ӨнҠёлҰӯмҠӨ В· cross-product matrix В· coverage м¶”м Ғ В· лӢӨмқҢ м…Җ В· м–ҙл”” мұ„мҡё м°ЁлЎҖ.

plugins.name: trail
plugins.description: Main-flow return stack вҖ” `/trail` is a LIFO deviation stack that records where you were on the MAIN FLOW (the active goal) when you pivot into ANY side-task вҖ” an intra-repo tangent/sub-fix OR a cross-repo/upstream fix вҖ” so the parent goal is never stranded. Deviations are NOT only cross-repo; a same-repo tangent off the active milestone strands the main flow just as easily. The store is HOME-global (`~/.sidecar/trail.tape`) вҖ” NOT cwd-local like `/draft` вҖ” so one stack tracks the current dive across intra-repo deviations AND survives `cd` when a detour does cross repos (e.g. anima DECODER вҶ’ hexa-lang flame вҶ’ codegen fix). Verbs вҖ” `push <return-target>` appends a frame (UTC timestamp + git-toplevel repo basename + return-target text) В· `pop` removes the top frame and prints the next frame to resume (or 'back on MAIN FLOW' when empty) В· bare/`show` renders the ladder with the deepest frame `вҳ… NOW` at top and the root at the bottom В· `clear` wipes В· `help`. LLM AUTO-USE (commons g74 companion) вҖ” SKILL.md instructs the calling LLM to run `/trail push` the instant work pivots into any side-task (intra-repo tangent/sub-fix or cross-repo/upstream fix) and `/trail pop` the instant it closes, no explicit command, plus `/trail` (show) on long yak-shaves to re-surface the climb-back path. Distinct from `/draft` (cwd-local throwaway) and `/domain` (in-repo milestone SSOT). Portable POSIX script (no GNU-only flags) so it runs identically on macOS + Linux pool hosts. Triggers: /trail В· нҠёл Ҳмқј В· ліөк·Җ мҠӨнғқ В· мқҙнғҲ кё°лЎқ В· л©”мқё нқҗлҰ„ н‘ңмӢң В· breadcrumb В· return stack В· м–ҙл””к№Ңм§Җ н–ҲлҚ”лқј В· мӣҗлһҳ лӯҗ н•ҳлҚҳ В· кіҒк°Җм§Җ В· detour В· climb back В· ліё нқҗлҰ„ ліөк·Җ.

plugins.name: end
plugins.description: Session closure safety check вҖ” /end prints a single-shot dashboard of dangling residue in the current repo (uncommitted В· unpushed В· stash В· open PRs by `@me` В· marketplace.json вҶ” plugin.json version drift В· merged-but-undeleted local branches В· linked git worktrees) with per-item вң“/вҡ /в—Ӣ marks + recommended actions, and a closing вң… `100% closure вҖ” safe to end` verdict only when every line is green. Read-only вҖ” no commit / push / branch delete / worktree remove. Pairs with /ship (the actor) вҖ” /end is the verifier. Triggers: /end В· лҒқ В· л§Ҳл¬ҙлҰ¬ В· мў…лЈҢ В· нҒҙлЎңм Җ В· close out В· wrap up В· 100% closure.

plugins.name: all-bg-go
plugins.description: Parallel fan-out trigger вҖ” 'all bg go' вҶ’ plan table + one background Agent per branch in the same message. REACTIVE single fan-out of what the PRIOR turn offered. For a self-generating repeatable loop, use /cycle instead. Skill (natural language) + /all-bg-go command + /abg 3-char alias (0.4.2).

plugins.name: all-fg-go
plugins.description: Foreground-sequential fan-out trigger вҖ” 'all fg go' вҶ’ plan table + run the prior turn's branches ONE AT A TIME in the foreground (await each, `в–¶ i/N вҶ’ вң…/вҡ /вқҢ`, halt on failure). REACTIVE single fan-out of what the PRIOR turn offered вҖ” the sequential sibling of all-bg-go (parallel background). No parallelism, no background fan-out; pick it when each branch must be watched in order. For a self-generating repeatable foreground loop, use /cycle-fg. Skill (natural language) + /all-fg-go command + /afg 3-char alias.

plugins.name: cycle
plugins.description: Autonomous work-loop driver scoped to the session's ACTIVE DOMAIN (commons @D g58). The WHOLE family now auto-continues to DEPLETION: each round self-feeds from open milestones (else auto-seeds from `## deferred`), self-continues via ScheduleWakeup, and terminates only when open milestones = 0 AND deferred empty AND no other signal вҖ” so every command digs the declared backlog to the bottom on its own (per-round cap throttles WIDTH, auto-continue provides DEPTH; user can interrupt any round). The commands: /cycle вҖ” read the active <NAME>.md snapshot, enumerate its open `- [ ]` milestones (off-domain work is forbidden; if no active domain, prompt for `/domain set <NAME>` first); if the next-list is empty, auto-seed вүӨN (default 3) candidate milestones вҖ” PRIMARY signal = the domain's OWN `## deferred` section (promote the next batch into the milestone board + drain it from deferred so the domain self-feeds its declared backlog), then user hint В· prior-turn /gap shortlist В· log-tail open thread вҖ” via `/domain milestone <text>` per item, then re-enumerate (no-signal case = zero milestones + empty deferred + no signal stops with steer-options rather than fabricating); вҶ’ dup-race precheck (3-signal grep: `sidecar handoff` registry entry status + merged PR + git log) вҶ’ parallel-plan table вҶ’ fan-out (one background Agent per item, same message) вҶ’ auto-continue to depletion (depletion-driving by default, not single-round-then-stop). /cycle-full вҖ” one-time phase-0 depletion brainstorm (rounds until no novel ideas, cap 8) on $ARGUMENTS within the active domain, then the same precheck вҶ’ plan-table вҶ’ fan-out вҶ’ auto-continue tail (phase 0 is one-time per goal; subsequent rounds use plain /cycle semantics, self-feeding from deferred, draining to depletion). /cycle-fg-loop вҖ” /cycle-fg handed to the `loop` skill for explicit continuous FOREGROUND-SEQUENTIAL intent + dynamic ScheduleWakeup pacing; the continuous-loop version of single-round /cycle-fg (each round runs inline one-at-a-time, в–¶ i/N вҶ’ вң…/вҡ /вқҢ, no Agent fan-out, halt on failure); same depletion termination. /cycle-bg-loop вҖ” /cycle-bg handed to the `loop` skill for explicit continuous BACKGROUND-PARALLEL intent + dynamic ScheduleWakeup pacing; the continuous-loop version of single-round /cycle-bg (each round fans out one background Agent per item, resource-serialized); same depletion termination. (Bare /cycle reaches the same end-state inline, dispatching fg/bg per the sticky ~/.sidecar/cycle-mode marker вҖ” the -loop commands are the explicit-pacing surface that names the execution mode.) /cycle-full-loop вҖ” /cycle-full once (one-time brainstorm + first fan-out) then handed to /loop /cycle, draining to the same depletion condition. /cycle-all (0.8.2) вҖ” like /cycle but with TWO overrides: NO per-round cap (enumerate the FULL open-milestone set + promote the ENTIRE `## deferred` backlog in one round, the `cap>8вҶ’confirm` guardrail waived) and NO recommend/select gate (do NOT print a 'recommended priority' shortlist or a 'which to dispatch?' hand-back вҖ” fan out EVERY PROCEED row immediately). For 'run everything' intent (м „л¶Җ м§„н–ү) instead of a curated вүӨN batch. /cycle-fg + /cycle-bg (0.9.1) вҖ” STICKY EXECUTION-MODE TOGGLES. `/cycle-fg` sets the session marker `~/.sidecar/cycle-mode=fg` and runs the round FOREGROUND SEQUENTIAL (Stages 0-3 unchanged; Stage 4 executes each PROCEED row INLINE one-at-a-time with visible `в–¶ i/N вҶ’ вң…/вҡ /вқҢ` progress В· NO Agent fan-out В· NO parallelism В· halt on failure В· sbs-like bar). `/cycle-bg` sets the marker `=bg` and runs the round in the default BACKGROUND mode (parallel Agent fan-out + auto-continue to depletion). The marker is STICKY: after `/cycle-fg`, subsequent bare `/cycle` rounds ALSO run foreground until `/cycle-bg` flips back (and vice-versa). Bare `/cycle` reads the marker at its Stage -1 (default `bg` when absent) and dispatches to the matching execution mode. fg = debugging В· careful review В· cwd-shared / single-threaded-resource work; bg (default) = independent parallel work-bursts that need isolation. Both keep ALL guardrails (SSOT-freshness В· dup-race SKIP В· leak-sweep В· resource-serialization). All safety/correctness guardrails are KEPT (SSOT-freshness, dup-race SKIP, leak-sweep, resource-contention serialization вҖ” fanning out everything still serializes EXCLUSIVE GPU resources, never parallel-fires every Agent вҖ” throttle-resilience, auto-continue to depletion). All commands share the 5-stage structure + plan-table + dup-race precheck + leak guardrails; they differ only in entry/pacing/cap/execution-mode shape. Distinct from all-bg-go (reactive fan-out of PRIOR-turn branches; /cycle self-generates the next batch each round). 0.8.0 fixes the per-round shape + adds THE LINTER (@D round_lint): every loop round ends with a result-report (status glyph + PR# + honest verify tier + 7-element easy + progress bar) вҶ’ debt/split tracking вҶ’ a `round-lint: N/10 вң“` self-audit of the 10-point contract вҶ’ loop tail; plus @D oversized_split (вүҘ2 consecutive throttle deaths on one milestone вҮ’ split into independently-landable sub-slices, never re-fire whole) + @D handoff_debt_ledger (cross-repo handoff goes to the host-local `sidecar handoff` registry вҖ” `sidecar handoff add <repo> <text>`, no target-tree write, so no uncommitted-edit debt). cycle-fg-loop/cycle-bg-loop/cycle-full-loop carry the fixed round shape. NL triggers: /cycle В· /cycle-full В· /cycle-fg-loop В· /cycle-bg-loop В· /cycle-full-loop В· /cycle-all В· /cycle-fg В· /cycle-bg В· мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ В· кі„мҶҚ м§„н–ү В· лӢӨмқҢ лқјмҡҙл“ң В· keep cycling В· march on В· м „л¶Җ м§„н–ү В· м¶”мІң л§җкі  м „л¶Җ В· мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ нҸ¬к·ёлқјмҡҙл“ң В· мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ мҲңм°Ё В· мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ м§Ғм ‘ В· foreground cycle В· sequential cycle В· inline cycle В· н•ң лӢЁкі„м”© мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ В· мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ л°ұк·ёлқјмҡҙл“ң В· мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ лі‘л ¬ В· background cycle В· parallel cycle В· bg лӘЁл“ңлЎң В· мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ нҸ¬к·ёлқјмҡҙл“ң лЈЁн”„ В· fg loop В· м—°мҶҚ мҲңм°Ё мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ В· мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ л°ұк·ёлқјмҡҙл“ң лЈЁн”„ В· bg loop В· м—°мҶҚ лі‘л ¬ мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ. 0.9.2 cross-links the /cycle-bg vs /micro-exp build-vs-fire decision tree (domain-agnostic; mirrored in skills/micro-exp/SKILL.md @D micro_exp_handoff): /cycle-bg is the canonical BUILD lane for candidate orchestration (build+fire integrated per worktree agent), /micro-exp is the FIRE-only lane (pre-built infra assumed; /micro-exp's Stage 1.5 auto-halts back to /cycle-bg on any missing prereq). Tree (kind-abstract): NO matrix вҶ’ /kick В· matrix + ALL infra ready вҶ’ /micro-exp В· matrix + missing infra вҶ’ /cycle-bg <domain> then /micro-exp.

plugins.name: pool
plugins.description: /pool <args> вҖ” wraps the `pool` CLI (host roster + remote exec вҖ” minimal, zero deps). Verbs: list В· add <host> В· on <host> <cmd> В· status В· install tailscale В· rm <host>. NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g9 + sidecar's pool-route hook.

plugins.name: cloud
plugins.description: /cloud <args> вҖ” wraps `hexa cloud` (runpod / vast.ai dispatch В· canonical subcommand form, structured argv вҖ” never raw runpodctl/vastai/ssh/scp/curl per commons g8, hard-enforced by the cloud-guard hook). Subverbs: preflight В· run В· nohup В· fire В· poll В· tail В· copy-to В· copy-from В· **pods В· dispatch**. preflight = closed-form GPU mem-budget check (refuses out-of-budget specs before pod spinup В· no LLM В· $0). tail = live remote-log stream вҶ’ Monitor bridge (ssh tail -F | sed -u В· terminal-exit on a crash-aware default В· commons g57). **`pods`/`dispatch` (new in hexa-lang PR #1699)** = per-project active pod/job work manifest at cwd's `./pods.json` (one file per repo вҖ” demiurge/anima/hexa-lang each carry their own dispatch state); `cloud dispatch [tree|active|add|verdict|rm]` manages it update-form. Distinct from `cloud orphans`/`reconcile` (which scan GLOBAL `~/.hexa-cloud/pods.jsonl` billing/orphan registry auto-tracked by `cloud run`/`nohup`) вҖ” `pods`/`dispatch` is the operator's manual work view per project. Atomic write + .bak rotation + auto `last_updated_utc` stamp (s11). NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g8 + g12 + g57.

plugins.name: hexa-help
plugins.description: /hexa-help [verb] вҖ” wraps `hexa --help` (no arg, top-level catalog) or `hexa <verb> --help` (verb-specific). Per commons g7. NL trigger via skill.

plugins.name: hexa
plugins.description: /hexa <verb> [args...] вҖ” generic passthrough to the hexa CLI (`hexa $ARGUMENTS`). Covers the 80+ verbs without a dedicated wrapper; /kick /atlas /verify /cloud take the hot paths. Guarded passthrough: dedicated-wrapper verbs (cloudВ·deckВ·kickВ·atlasВ·verify) are refused + redirected to their dedicated skill so guards/preflight aren't bypassed. Routed to pool by pool-route. NL trigger via skill.

plugins.name: secret
plugins.description: /secret <args> вҖ” wraps the `secret` CLI (macOS Keychain-backed credentials, dancinlab/secret 0.4.0, dual-channel sync). Verbs: get В· set В· rotate В· check В· delete В· list В· service В· init [icloud|github <url>] В· backup [enable <url>|disable|status] В· sync В· migrate. Two independent sync channels (iCloud Drive primary + optional private GitHub mirror) push the same encrypted blob вҖ” master password is the only decryption secret. Auto-push ON by default once `backup enable` runs (opt out via SECRET_BACKUP_AUTO=0). High-value protection on set (BIP39 wordlist-validated В· xprv/WIF/64-hex auto-detect вҶ’ refuse without --allow-mnemonic + stdin/tty). rotate emits sentinel only.

plugins.name: quota
plugins.description: /quota:quota <args> вҖ” Claude account 5h/7d usage limits + multi-account registry + live credential swap + per-account nicknames. Single-dispatch picker entry; verb routed via $ARGUMENTS. Verbs: list (= status = bare В· UNIFIED all-accounts table in chat, 6-col English header: Nickname В· Email В· Session Used В· Session Reset В· Weekly Used В· Weekly Reset, в–“в–‘ 5-slot bar + humanized time-to-reset; rows sorted by nickname (else email), case-insensitive; the active account is вҳ… + fetched LIVE (45s cache), every other account shows its last per-account cache, `вҖ”` = never fetched) В· all (same table, every account fetched LIVE in one shot, in PARALLEL вҖ” the easy toggle to 'refresh everything') В· add [<nick>] (snapshot account + capture credentials into backup store В· optional nickname like claude1) В· nick <ref> [<nick>] (set or clear a nickname for an existing account; bare nick arg = clear) В· switch <ref> (live cred swap, verified + rollback on failure) В· remove <ref> (rm В· delete В· del вҖ” drop registry row + backup creds, leaves live ~/.claude.json untouched) В· refresh [<ref>] (re-fetch usage into per-account cache; renews expired tokens via refreshToken) В· autoadd (silent SessionStart-hook path вҖ” registers the active account iff not yet in the registry; driven by the quota-autoadd hook plugin so a fresh `claude /login` self-registers next session) В· help. The view also auto-registers the live account if untracked (autoadd parity). <ref> = nickname (e.g. claude1) В· email В· numeric index вҖ” lookup order indexвҶ’nickвҶ’email. Live fetch via OAuth usage endpoint (api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage) + auto refresh-token grant (platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token); 45s per-account cache. Per-account OAuth blob backup in macOS keychain svc 'sidecar-quota' / linux ~/.sidecar/quota/creds/<email>.json (0600 + atomic write). own-accounts-serial only вҖ” never a multi-tenant router (ToS red-line). Agent SDK credit (dual-bucket since 2026-06-15) is documented as future direction; current release tracks subscription 5h/7d only. The bare/list view renders from cache instantly then spawns a detached background stale-while-revalidate refresh of stale non-active accounts (cache > 300s, with backup creds) so the NEXT view trends fresh without an explicit `all` вҖ” the view never blocks on the network; a dead/expired backup token is skipped for a 1800s cooldown (shows `вҖ”` until `refresh <ref>` renews it); the active account keeps a 45s inline live refresh. Both `all` and the background cycle fetch their account set in parallel (`_parallel_refresh` вҖ” token-prep sequential, usage GETs concurrent). вҡ  uses non-public Anthropic OAuth endpoints вҖ” may break if rotated.

plugins.name: quota-autoadd
plugins.description: SessionStart hook вҖ” silently auto-registers the active Claude account into the quota registry when a fresh `claude /login` introduces an account not yet tracked. Invokes the quota skill's `_quota.hexa autoadd` (idempotent В· always exit 0 В· prints one line only when a NEW account lands; already-registered sessions are fully silent). The add path also captures the OAuth blob into the backup store so `/quota:quota switch` works for the new account immediately. Pairs with the `quota` skill вҖ” quota-autoadd is the default-on registration layer (manual `/quota:quota add` remains). Resolves the quota binary via $HOME/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/quota/<version>/bin/_quota.hexa (portable; $HOME-based; picks the latest cached version by semver `sort -V`, not mtime). NO opt-out by design (commons g11).

plugins.name: kick
plugins.description: /kick <natural-language seed> вҖ” runs `hexa kick --seed "<seed>"` (hexa-lang gap-breakthrough / discovery engine, aliased to `hexa drill`). All args join into the seed. NL trigger via skill (`kick this`, `лҸҢнҢҢн•ҙмӨҳ`, `discover for`). Pairs with commons g6.

plugins.name: verify
plugins.description: /verify <args> вҖ” runs `hexa verify "$@"` (cross-project tier rubric, TECS-L-aligned). Forms: <id> (atlas atom), --expr <fn> <n> <v> (numerical), --fence "<claim>" (honest вҡӘ), rubric / list. Returns рҹ”өрҹҹўрҹҹЎрҹҹ рҹ”ҙвҡӘ tier badge VERBATIM. NL trigger via skill (`verify this`, `нҷ•мқён•ҙ`, `кІҖмҰқн•ҙ`, `л§һм•„?`). Pairs with commons g5.

plugins.name: atlas
plugins.description:
/atlas вҖ” wraps `hexa atlas` (atlas SSOT surface). SSOT = `compiler/atlas/embedded.gen.hexa` (binary-builtin atlas, ~16088 nodes), served at runtime by static_atlas via TEXT-parse of embedded.gen.hexa. Read verbs: hash В· stats [--audit [--scope=rodata|overlay|merged] [--format=json]] В· lookup [K] / lookup --prefix=

(K вҲҲ P|C|L|E|F|R|S|X|Q) В· dump [K] [--json]. Write verb (ONE): register --from-verify (or 2-op) В· register --from-drill --seed "" [--rounds N] [--engine mk9|mk10] вҖ” runs the verb in-process and on рҹ”ө/рҹҹў folds the verified AtlasNode DIRECTLY into compiler/atlas/embedded.gen.hexa, visible to the next lookup with NO rebuild, NO staging shard, NO intermediate file (commit embedded.gen.hexa with normal git to share). Export verb (NEW): export [--out PATH] вҖ” writes the live atlas out as a portable .n6 file (default n6/atlas.n6, an export artifact only, NOT the runtime SSOT). NL trigger via skill (`atlas lookup`, `atlas stats`, `м•„нӢҖлқјмҠӨ мЎ°нҡҢ`, `м•„нӢҖлқјмҠӨ л“ұлЎқ`, `register from drill`, `atlas absorb`).

plugins.name: domain
plugins.description: Maintain UPPERCASE <NAME>.md (snapshot = final-goal milestone checkboxes) + sister <NAME>.log.md (append-only step log) at project root вҖ” auto-scaffolds both, defaults NAME to uppercase basename of git root. Verbs: /domain <NAME> (SELECT the session's active domain вҶ’ later verbs default to it + show a final-goal progress bar `в–“в–“в–“в–‘в–‘ NN% В· done/total` from the snapshot's checkboxes) В· /domain (show active) В· /domain list (alias ls вҖ” repo-wide index; with a DOMAINS.tape roster it is AUTHORITATIVE: tables each registered domain вҳ… = active В· @goal В· progress В· location, progress/@goal stay DERIVED so the checked-in roster holds only NAMEвҶ’path and never churns; flags ghosts + unregistered; no roster вҶ’ legacy disk-scan) В· /domain list --sync (reconcile DOMAINS.tape with disk вҖ” bootstraps the roster) В· /domain <task> (append `- [x]` to log) В· /domain todo <task> (`- [ ]`) В· /domain done <match> (flip [ ]вҶ’[x]) В· /domain title <text> (alias subtitle вҖ” OPTIONAL `@title:` display header: icon В· name В· alias, e.g. `рҹ§  IIT4 вҖ” мқҳмӢқ мёЎм •мһҗ(е°ә)`, rendered in place of plain `active domain: NAME`; absence keeps current output, no lint warning) В· /domain new <header>. Progress is final-goal-based (snapshot completion), not log-based вҖ” it doesn't drift as the log grows. Path resolution: a DOMAINS.tape roster row (`@domain NAME := "path"`) wins FIRST, so a domain can live at ANY path (e.g. `domains/RUNTIME/RUNTIME.md`); absent a roster entry it falls back to root вҶ’ folder-nested вҶ’ root-default-scaffold (log follows the snapshot's dir), so folder-nested meta-domains no longer regenerate an empty root scaffold each call. The folder-nested fallback only matches when the nested file's first line is the domain header `# <NAME>` (guards against false-matching an unrelated same-named file like a digest on case-insensitive filesystems). 0.8.8: /domain set now SELF-HEALS the DOMAINS.tape roster (auto-registers an on-disk-but-unregistered domain вҖ” the gap that ghosted anima's LIFE) and warns on a stale SSOT shadow (the working-tree <NAME>.md is UNTRACKED or BEHIND origin/main вҶ’ progress/closure may read a stale local copy; reference_domain_init_untracked_ssot); a perpetual `@goal` (no-termination marker like `мў…лЈҢ мЎ°кұҙ м—ҶмқҢ`/`perpetual`/`open horizon`) renders a вҷҫпёҸ perpetual badge so the progress bar reads as frontier-depletion, NOT a 100% completion countdown (per feedback-closure-is-physical-limit).

plugins.name: go
plugins.description: /go [hint] вҖ” proceed with the most-recently proposed action / continue the paused flow without further confirmation. Bare "go" single-word user message also catches as natural-language alias. Identical semantics to 'к·ёлғҘ м§„н–үн•ҙ' / 'continue' / 'just run it' вҖ” re-read the last agent message, identify what was proposed/paused/awaiting (a plan В· a recommended option В· the next /sbs step В· the next /cycle round В· a /cycle-fg halted item В· an AskUserQuestion default), and execute it verbatim. Optional `/go <hint>` disambiguates (e.g. `/go ubu-2` selects host, `/go retry` re-fires a failed step, `/go skip` advances past, `/go all` applies to every candidate). NO new plan, NO re-question вҖ” stateless continuation token. If no recent proposal exists, ask one short line rather than fabricate. Distinct from /sbs (plan-first sequential) and /cycle (autonomous fan-out): /go is a token, not a runbook. Triggers вҖ” '/go', bare 'go' (single-word), 'м§„н–ү', 'кі„мҶҚ', 'лӢӨмқҢ м§„н–ү', 'proceed', 'continue', 'ok go', 'yes go'.

plugins.name: roi
plugins.description: /roi [scope] вҖ” enumerate LOSSLESS performance/resource/speed improvement candidates as a ranked TODO list. "Lossless" = no functional regression, no API breakage, no semantic change, no test failures вҖ” only HOW the same behavior is achieved more efficiently. Scope: bare /roi = active domain (if set) ELSE cwd repo В· /roi <message> restricts to that scope (file path В· subdirectory В· feature name В· layer В· language В· hot path). Output is a single ranked table `| # | category | item | impact | effort | risk | how | evidence |` where category вҲҲ {вҡЎ speed В· рҹ§  perf (CPU/algo) В· рҹ’ҫ resource (mem/disk/network) В· рҹ”Ӣ efficiency}, sorted by impact-to-effort ratio (highest ROI first), risk=low ONLY (any candidate that could risk behavior change is dropped вҖ” that's NOT lossless). Discovery: scope read вҶ’ anti-pattern grep (N+1, re-parse-per-call, O(nВІ) where O(n log n) trivially applies, unbounded growth, sync I/O on hot path, sort+take-1 vs min, regex recompile per loop, dict lookup not hoisted, вҖҰ) вҶ’ bench-ground claims where measurable (@D bench_kernel_choices, к°Җм • л§җкі  лІӨм№ҳ) вҶ’ rank. Cap default top 10/category. Drop-count surfaced when items excluded for riskвүҘmedium. Triggers вҖ” '/roi', '/roi <scope>', 'roi лҪ‘м•„', 'roi list', 'л¬ҙмҶҗмӢӨ к°ңм„  н• мқј', 'м„ұлҠҘ к°ңм„  н• мқј', 'мһҗмӣҗ к°ңм„  н• мқј', 'мҶҚлҸ„ к°ңм„  н• мқј', 'нҡЁмңЁ к°ңм„  н• мқј', 'lossless improvements', 'perf wins'.

plugins.name: gap
plugins.description: /gap вҖ” multi-axis gap exploration. 42 breakthrough-strategy lenses В· 8 families (F4 + F6 each have 6 lenses; `occams-razor` lives in both вҖ” hypothesis side and design side). Bare /gap = inline-triage + deep-dive only hot families. /gap full = exhaustive 8-subagent fan-out. /gap <scope> targets the sweep. /gap list prints the catalogue. Surfaces + prioritises gaps; never fixes.

plugins.name: sidecar
plugins.description: /sidecar <args> вҖ” thin wrapper over the `sidecar` marketplace CLI (host-local, on PATH via `hx install sidecar`). 0.8.0 вҖ” NEW `handoff` verb: the cross-project handoff registry (~/.sidecar/handoff/handoff.jsonl, single SSOT for cross-repo gap/patch/bug/RFC handoffs per commons g11В·g36В·g48В·g59В·g60; retires the per-repo INBOX domain). `handoff add <repo> <text...>` appends an open JSONL entry В· `handoff ls [<repo>]` tables entries (filter by repo) В· `handoff done <id>` flips status вҶ’ done В· bare `handoff` prints a counts summary. JSONL not markdown (structured carrier В· project.tape s2); store is repo-external runtime state, portable via $HOME; atomic tmp+rename writes. 0.7.0 вҖ” `reap` verb (reap stale `hexa-lsp` idle > 2h; dry-run by default, `reap --kill` terminates; never touches claude @D s9 or active hexa runs) + `paths add/rm` SIGN-GATED (key `paths`: `! sidecar sign paths` first, since the whitelist overrides pool-route). Verbs вҖ” init (scaffold project.tape + CLAUDE.md symlink + LATTICE_POLICY.md) В· sync (pull marketplace + refresh cache + installed_plugins.json + enable plugins + mirror commands) В· mirror (refresh ~/.claude/commands/ mirrors so plugin commands are bare-invocable вҖ” `Skill("imagine")` instead of `Skill("imagine:imagine")` вҖ” auto-runs at sync tail) В· shadow [plan] (drop the FORCED `plugin:command` namespace #15882 вҖ” mirror+disable every command/skill plugin, splicing SKILL.md triggers into the bare command so it stays lossless; hook/mcp kept; `plan` = dry-run; re-asserted at sync tail) В· unshadow (revert) В· paths (0.6.0 NEW вҖ” manage the LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST `~/.sidecar/local-paths`; pool-route 0.10.0 forces any Bash cmd whose argv contains a `/`-prefixed token starting with a registered prefix to run LOCAL. `paths` lists, `paths add` registers cwd, `paths add <dir>` registers an abs-resolved dir (refuses non-existent / already-present), `paths rm <prefix>` removes a line by exact match. Seeded on first use with `~/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/` + `~/core/sidecar/`. Atomic writes; whitelist itself IS the opt-in вҖ” no env opt-out вҖ” @D s11 safe.) В· sign (user sign-off gate). `sign <key>` mints a 5-min token at ~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign unlocking agent edits to a sign-gated governance SSOT file вҖ” the sign-guard hook hard-denies those edits until a fresh token exists; keys are `commons` (commons.tape), `project` (project.tape), `gitignore` (.gitignore), and `paths` (~/.sidecar/local-paths). Bare `sign` / `sign --list` shows active tokens; `sign clear [<key>]` removes them. Multi-user enable profiles вҖ” `profile [<name>]` shows/sets the enable profile (minimal = core only В· hexa = core + hexa-lang В· full = everything, default В· master = full + creator-only). `master [on|off|status]` mints/removes the ~/.sidecar/master marker. `enable`/`disable <plugin>` force one plugin on/off (per-plugin override), `reset <plugin>` drops the override; each re-applies the install. State in ~/.sidecar/profile + plugin-overrides.json; install.hexa enables per .claude-plugin/profiles.json tiers вҖ” plugin disable, not an in-guard opt-out (@D s11 safe). Args pass through unchanged.

plugins.name: inject
plugins.description: Immediately inject the latest sidecar commons.tape + project.tape into the CURRENT session and sync the local install for the next session. /inject (and `/ij` short alias) runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch) AND prints the current commons.tape + (cwd's) project.tape so the model picks them up this turn.

plugins.name: ship
plugins.description: Atomic ship tail for sidecar plugin changes вҖ” /ship -m "<msg>" <path>... stages explicit paths (never -A/-u), credential-scans the staged diff (rpa_ В· sk- В· hf_ В· AKIA), commits, pushes origin/<branch>, then runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). Mechanical tail of @D ship / commons g27; the agent owns version bump + surface lockstep + message FIRST. Bare /ship (no -m / no paths) is helper mode (0.3.2): lists uncommitted changes + emits a ready-to-edit `/ship -m "..." <paths>` template instead of a usage error, exits 0, still never auto-stages. NL trigger via skill (ship В· л°°нҸ¬ В· м¶ңмӢң).

plugins.name: bypass
plugins.description: Anti-punt вҖ” DEFAULT BEHAVIOR (auto-fires, not opt-in). Universal self-check before any move that hands control back to the user (interactive input В· unauthorized destructive В· external visible В· explicit user-review request); if all NO, just execute. Extensible catalog of patterns: 'next user action:' blocks В· 'Should I proceed?' confirmations В· 'Want me to check?' deferrals В· option-trees with obvious default В· over-clarification В· defer-by-waiting В· excessive recap. Cross-project always-on enforcement in commons.tape вүҘ0.7.3.

plugins.name: brainstorm
plugins.description: Iterative brainstorming вҖ” given a seed, generates ideas in rounds and keeps going until depletion (no genuinely new ideas vs prior rounds; hard cap 8 rounds). For breadth over selection. Skill (natural language) + /brainstorm command.

plugins.name: easy

plugins.name: easy-auto
plugins.description: SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit + PreCompact + PostCompact hook вҖ” auto-injects the `easy` (friendly) 7-element response style as additionalContext so the pattern is active from turn 0 without the user calling /easy. Reads ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/styles/easy.<lang>.md (env `EASY_LANG` override, default `ko`); falls back to easy.md (English base) when the lang variant is missing. UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the style never fades; PreCompact + PostCompact survive auto-compaction. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_easy_auto.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Pairs with the `easy` skill (manual /easy trigger remains вҖ” easy-auto is the default-on layer). `EASY_LANG` only SELECTS the style language (default `ko`) вҖ” not a disable switch. NO opt-out by design (commons @D g11 / sidecar @D s11); 0.1.2 removed the `EASY_LANG=off` suppress path. 0.2.0 вҖ” н•ңкөӯм–ҙ substring нҠёлҰ¬кұ° 'м„ӨлӘ…'/'мүҪкІҢ' м¶”к°Җ (UserPromptSubmit мӢң verb/noun/adverb лӘЁл“  м–ҙнҳ• catch: 'м„ӨлӘ…н•ҙ'/'м„ӨлӘ…н•ҙмӨҢ'/'м„ӨлӘ…'/'м„ӨлӘ…м„ң'/'мүҪкІҢ'/'мүҪкІҢ м•Ңл ӨмӨҢ' л“ұ; мҳӨнғҗ к°ҖлҠҘм„ұ мҲҳмҡ©) + л°ңлҸҷ мӢң 1мӨ„ banner emit ('рҹҺ“ easy лӘЁл“ң нҷңм„ұ вҖ” 7-мҡ”мҶҢ нҢЁн„ҙ м Ғмҡ© вҖҰ'). кё°мЎҙ injectлҠ” к·ёлҢҖлЎң always-on (trigger gating м•„лӢҳ вҖ” bannerл§Ң м¶”к°Җ).

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: Research-fetch tools вҖ” `/research:arxiv <query|id>` searches the official arXiv API or fetches by id (title В· authors В· abstract В· pdf); `/research:yt <url-or-id>` extracts YouTube caption transcript via the InnerTube ANDROID client. plugins.name: gh-stack

plugins.name: paper

Verbs: list [--sync] В· set <slug> В· show [<slug>] В· goal <text> В· title <text> В· ms <text> В· done <match> В· todo <text> В· new <slug> В· sample [<name>] [<dest>] В· samples В· monograph-init <slug> [N] В· fill <letter> В· fig <size> <prompt> <out.png> В· compile [dir] В· lint [dir] В· companion {init|sync} В· outline [dir] В· pipeline ... В· atoms <domain> В· verify-block ... В· bib add <id> В· pr-roll <OWNER/REPO> <since> В· arxiv-prep [<dir>] В· help. v0.11 fixes (INBOX #112 from demiurge ANTIMATTER): (1) pr-roll вҖ” early reject for bare repo name + jq `\\#` escape so LaTeX `\#` survives; (2) atoms вҖ” LC_ALL=C around BSD awk + lowercase compare + matches `fn <name>(` dispatch lines; (3) arxiv-prep вҖ” resolves dir to absolute path before tar subshell. v0.10 PAPER.tape roster + per-paper PAPER.md/log.md unchanged.

plugins.name: imagine
plugins.description: /imagine <prompt-file> <out.png> [-s size] [-b backend] [-m model] вҖ” generic AI image generator. Two backends out of the box: `fal` (queue+poll fal.ai, default вҖ” `secret get fal.api_key`, model = openai/gpt-image-2 firm-pinned default) and `openai` (sync /v1/images/generations вҖ” `secret get openai.api_key`, model = gpt-image-1). plugins.name: output-trim
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) stdout trimmer вҖ” when a simple single Bash command would emit >8000 chars, transparently rewrite via `updatedInput` (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput вҖ” REQUIRED, else Claude Code ignores the rewrite and stdout is never trimmed; the 0.1.3 fix) to pipe stdout through a minimal hexa-native kernel: consecutive-line dedup ([Г—N] counter) + per-line truncation to 200 chars. Small outputs <8000 chars pass verbatim. Skipped on any shell metacharacter so compound pipelines / redirects / heredocs / background jobs / command substitutions and other PreToolUse(Bash) rewrite hooks compose without conflict. Exit code preserved via `set -o pipefail`. Kernel chosen by benchmark on 120 real Bash-stdout samples вүҘ8000 chars вҖ” Pareto-dominates the TF-IDF salience + MinHash near-dup paragraph dedup approach of the original `wilson-output-trim`. 0.1.4 вҖ” dead retired-rule source citation in plugin.json replaced with the current no-opt-out rule (project.tape @D s11). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: subagent-route
plugins.description: [POC] PreToolUse(Task|Agent) observation hook + SessionStart observation-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_subagent_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). v0.1.0 is observation-only вҖ” it does NOT deny, NOT rewrite. On every Task/Agent fire it dumps `tool_input` (key list + agent_type + prompt preview + isolation) to `~/.sidecar/subagent-route.log.jsonl` (capped 100 lines, jsonl format mirroring pool-route's route-log) and emits a non-blocking `additionalContext` advisory suggesting the equivalent `ssh <host> -- claude --print --no-tty -p '<prompt>'` rewrite. SessionStart event reads the last 5 observations back as additionalContext so the model sees recent subagent fires without recon. PoC purpose вҖ” probe the actual tool_input payload shape at runtime (Claude Code docs leave Agent/Task tool_input fields under-specified vs Bash), and probe the `additionalContext` UX before a real-rewrite v0.2.0. Always exits 0; advisory only.

plugins.name: worktree-gc
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that prunes merged-but-undeleted LINKED git worktrees in the cwd repo, implemented in hexa-lang (`_worktree_gc.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Threshold gate (linked-count >= 3 OR disk avail < 20 GiB) keeps the common case (1-2 worktrees, ample disk) silent. Above threshold, fetches `gh pr list --state merged --limit 200` and considers a worktree only when its branch is in that merged set OR its HEAD commit truly landed on origin/main. 0.2.0 adds FOUR unconditional live-work guards so an active worktree is NEVER wiped mid-task: (a) dirty-tree (uncommitted changes вҶ’ SKIP), (b) recent-mtime (HEAD commit or any file modified < 1h вҶ’ SKIP), (c) cwd-in-use (a live process' cwd is in the worktree, via lsof вҶ’ SKIP), (d) HEAD-ancestor real-merged check (`git merge-base --is-ancestor <wt-HEAD> origin/main`) вҖ” a vanished `origin/<branch>` ref alone is IGNORED so a reused branch name can't condemn fresh work. (e) Atomic prune: `git worktree remove` is the sole removal path; on failure NOTHING is deleted (no partial / manual rm). Open-PR worktrees are NEVER touched. gh unavailable / no merged PRs вҶ’ short note + no action. Always exits 0 (non-blocking). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: sidecar-auto-sync
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that runs `sidecar sync` once per Claude Code session, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_auto_sync.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Closes the gap between an upstream `git push` on origin/main and the local plugin cache вҖ” no more manual `sidecar sync` between sessions. Silent on success; brief stderr advisory on failure. Skipped silently only on a real precondition: the `sidecar` binary not on PATH (fresh machine without `hx install sidecar` yet). Adds ~1-2s to session startup (network pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11) вҖ” no env var / config disable switch; uninstall the plugin for manual control.

plugins.name: s9-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for load-assessment commands (project.tape @D s9). Fires when a Bash command looks like a Mac load check вҖ” `uptime` (word) В· `loadavg` / `vm.loadavg` (substring) В· `ps -A` adjacency or `ps -Ao pcpu` В· `top -l ` (macOS) / `top -b ` (Linux) В· `getloadavg`. Emits non-blocking systemMessage reminding the agent that any conclusion drawn from raw load must EXCLUDE `claude` PIDs (claude sessions are exempt from local load attribution) and suggesting the canonical claude-excluded sum (`ps -Ao pcpu,comm | awk '$2 !~ /claude/ {s+=$1} END {print int(s)}'`). NEVER blocks вҖ” pure advisory. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_s9_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Ships per @D s7 (governance + enforcement together) вҖ” the @D s9 companion guard. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: askq-text
plugins.description: PreToolUse(AskUserQuestion) вҶ’ plain-text chat redirect, in hexa-lang (`_askq_text.hexa`, via `hexa run`). AskUserQuestion renders an arrow-key option-tree box in the TUI; this host prefers plain CHAT вҖ” questions asked as ordinary text, answered conversationally. A PreToolUse hook cannot re-render the box as text (no transform API), so the hook DENIES the call with an instruction telling the model to re-emit the SAME question as plain text in its reply (state it in prose, list any options inline marking a recommendation, accept a free-form answer). A FORM redirect, NOT anti-punt вҖ” the model SHOULD still ask, just in chat rather than the box (distinct from the `bypass` skill, which is about NOT asking clearly-OK reversible work). Plan approval keeps ExitPlanMode and is NOT touched. Unconditional вҖ” the tool name IS the precondition, NO env-var / flag escape hatch (project.tape @D s11). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: lsp-rebuild
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) auto-rebuild for hexa-native LSP servers, in hexa-lang (`_lsp_rebuild.hexa`, via `hexa run`). When the agent edits an LSP grammar source matching `*/lsp/<lang>_lsp.hexa`, the prebuilt `~/.local/bin/<lang>-lsp-hexa` is stale (next LSP session would run the old grammar), so the hook rebuilds it in the BACKGROUND вҖ” `nohup env HEXA_MAC_BUILD_OK=1 hexa build <src> -o ~/.local/bin/<lang>-lsp-hexa` (detached `&` вҶ’ PostToolUse returns immediately; output вҶ’ ~/.sidecar/lsp-rebuild.log) вҖ” and emits a non-blocking advisory. Keeps the build artifact in lockstep with the canonical .hexa grammar SSOT so a grammar change auto-propagates without a manual rebuild. Rebuild-not-source-run because the hexa LSP stdio server needs the `read_stdin_n_c` FFI which only links into a COMPILED binary. Fires on the `*/lsp/*_lsp.hexa` pattern AND on hexa-lang's own LSP (`self/lsp.hexa` monolith + `self/lsp/*.hexa` submodules вҖ” NOT named `*_lsp.hexa`), rebuilt via its `tool/build_hexa_lsp.hexa` orchestrator вҶ’ `<repo>/bin/hexa-lsp` (project.tape @D s11). Pairs with the `*-lsp` wiring plugins (hexa-lsp В· kosmos-lsp В· n6-lsp В· hxc-lsp). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: step-by-step
plugins.description: /step-by-step (alias /sbs) вҖ” plan-first sequential runbook with TWO modes (auto В· manual default В· 0.5.0 2-mode redesign) + 0.6.0 мһҗлҸҷ 4м¶• QA after ship (functionalВ·visibleВ·conformanceВ·regression В· regression FAIL вҶ’ auto-revert В· лӮҳлЁём§Җ alert В· SKIP=PASS-equivalent В· кІ°кіјлҠ” plan.md `## qa-results`). MANUAL (the DEFAULT) вҖ” chat-form disambiguation: ONE question per round in chat (NOT AskUserQuestion), each round framed with the easy-mode 7-element scaffold (icon В· canonical name В· friendly alias В· plain-line В· everyday analogy В· ASCII sketch В· option compare-table В· one-line recommendation) and closed with `вҶ’ A В· B В· лҳҗлҠ” мһҗмң мқ‘лӢө` (free-form answers explicitly OK). Repeat round-by-round until ambiguity = 0, THEN render a `рҹҺҜ н•©мқҳлҗң кІ°м •м…Ӣ (Nк°ң)` ASCII tree of every accumulated decision and PAUSE вҖ” user types `go` to confirm or `Qn=<other>` to flip ONLY that one (re-render after each flip). On `go`, write `drafts/<slug>-plan.md` (gitignored; frontmatter slug/mode/auto-weights/created + task brief + locked decisions + next-action checklist ending with a ship line + completion criteria) and LAUNCH A BACKGROUND AGENT (general-purpose, run_in_background=true) with a self-contained prompt вҖ” the agent runs end-to-end including ship + auto-QA 4м¶•, no further user input needed; user can leave. AUTO вҖ” same chat-form scaffold rendered, but each question is AUTO-PICKED by the 4-axis weighted average instead of waiting for the user: мҷ„м„ұлҸ„ (complete В· robust + edge-case coverage) + лӢЁмҲң (simple В· Occam) + м•Ҳм „ (safe В· blast radius minimized) + н‘ңмӨҖ (std В· fits the sidecar pattern), default 1:1:1:1, inline override `/sbs auto:safety <task>` (single-axis force) or `/sbs auto:complete=2,simple=3 <task>` (explicit weights). AUTO still pauses at the agreement screen so auto-picks get a user pre-commit checkpoint. Mid-run new ambiguity also opens a chat round + re-renders the agreement screen. Mode = FIRST arg token `auto`[:`<axis-or-weights>`] | `manual` (default manual). `legacy-manual` = the old per-step pause behavior вҖ” 1-version deprecation banner, use plain `manual` going forward. `go` is NOT a mode (use `/go` to continue the paused flow). ALL modes halt on a step failure (failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) and pause before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). 0.6.0 auto-QA 4м¶• (handoff agent ship м§Ғнӣ„ мһҗлҸҷ мӢӨн–ү): functional (мғҲ endpoint/verb мқ‘лӢө) В· visible (URL/path/surface л…ём¶ң) В· conformance (locked decision вҶ” мҪ”л“ң 1:1) В· regression (кё°мЎҙ surface лҜёмҶҗмғҒ). Hybrid fail м •мұ… вҖ” regression FAILл§Ң auto-revert (`git revert <SHA> && git push && sidecar sync`) + banner, лӮҳлЁём§Җ 3м¶•мқҖ ship мң м§Җ + plan.md `## qa-deferred` append + banner. ALL PASS/SKIP вҶ’ banner м—Ҷмқҙ plan.md `## qa-results`м—җ вң“ лқјмқёл§Ң append. 0.7.0 вҖ” ship+QA м§Ғнӣ„ мһҗлҸҷ HANDOFF.md 9-section мқёкі„ doc (PR л§ӨнҠёлҰӯмҠӨ В· SSOT нҢҢмқј мқёлҚұмҠӨ В· мғҲ API surface В· м»ҙнҸ¬л„ҢнҠё нҠёлҰ¬ В· env ліҖмҲҳ В· лӢӨмқҢ мҡ°м„ мҲңмң„ В· н•ңкі„+guard В· memory мқёлҚұмҠӨ В· н•ң мӨ„ мӢңмһ‘ к°Җмқҙл“ң; `memory/project_<slug>_handoff.md` лҜёлҹ¬ В· planм—җ handoff verb л©ҙ end-user dossier bundle лҸ„). 0.8.0 вҖ” plan.md frontmatter `status: active` stamp к·ңм•Ҫ + `## locked decisions` л¶ҲлҰҝ кё°кі„к°ҖлҸ… contract нҷ•мһҘ `- @L<n> (<axis>): <option> В· assert:<grep|file|verdict> <arg>` (grep <pat>В·`!pat`=л¶Җмһ¬ / file <path>В·мЎҙмһ¬ / verdict <slug>/<id>В·`.verdicts/` non-empty). мӢ к·ң `plan-guard` н”Ңлҹ¬к·ёмқёмқҙ мқҙ marker лҘј sub-agent н”„лЎ¬н”„нҠём—җ verbatim мЈјмһ… + ship мӢң `plan-lint` кІҖмҰқ; `@L` м—ҶлҠ” кё°мЎҙ plan.md лҠ” skip (нӣ„л°©нҳёнҷҳ). 0.8.1 вҖ” plan лқјмқҙн”„мӮ¬мқҙнҒҙ лӘ…мӢң: write мӢң frontmatter `status: active` stamp еҝ… (м •нҷ•нһҲ 1к°ңл§Ң active), closure мӢң `status: done`(нҸҗкё° мӢң `abandoned`)лЎң flip вҖ” plan-guard нҷңм„ұм¶”м Ғ(@L6)мқҙ stale plan мқҳ @L мқ„ мһҳлӘ» к°•м ңн•ҳм§Җ м•ҠкІҢ н•Ё.

plugins.name: deck
plugins.description: /deck <domain> <slug> '<spec-json>' вҖ” рҹҚһ лҸ„л©”мқё input deck л№өнӢҖ generator. Thin wrapper around `hexa-lang stdlib/deck/gen.hexa`. v0.2 вҖ” s3 portable-path fix: the Step-1 runnable now invokes `hexa run $HOME/.hx/install/hexa-lang/stdlib/deck/gen.hexa вҖҰ` instead of a hardcoded `/Users/ghost/...` absolute (pool-route 0.15.0 ships the companion `stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` LOCAL pin so the now-`/Users/`-free command still stays local). RTSC QE el-ph first impl (4 files emit: vc-relax.in В· scf.in В· ph.in В· run.sh; spec keys = anchor В· press В· q_grid В· sscha? В· soc?; PSL 1.0.0 pseudo auto-branch on `soc` flag). Post-emit report = 4-file size + closed-form preflight (`hexa cloud preflight --kind dft-phonon --atoms N --nq M`) + d16 governance hint (pool ubu-1 free 1-iter dry-run before cost-bearing rent В· catches QE directive/basis/pseudo errors free). plugins.name: hexa-loop
plugins.description: /hexa-loop [seed] [--adapter=atlas|bench|byte-diff|smoke] [--diverge-only] [--engine mk9|mk10] [--max-rounds N] вҖ” canonical HEXA-LOOP, the universal self-evolving loop. ONE loop drains ANY active domain (math В· perf В· codegen В· web) to genuine depletion via a PLUGGABLE verify-adapter. Each round = (1) DISCOVER вҖ” mining-style lens divergence + connect convergence saturate (the old /mining auto, now internal) PLUS a `hexa kick`/`hexa drill` breakthrough probe on the ranked top-3 candidates (the old mirror-loop 'miningвҶ’kickвҶ’' step, now internal), (2) рҹ”Ң VERIFY-ADAPTER auto-selected by active-domain kind вҖ” atlas (math: `hexa atlas register --from-drill` / `hexa verify`, fold to embedded.gen.hexa) В· bench (perf: measure вҶ’ byte-diff IDENTICAL + roofline % (achieved/binding-roof, binding = compute/memory roof auto-selected by arithmetic-intensity vs ridge-point; denominator = achieved-peak microbench, NOT spec-sheet) + О” vs baseline) В· byte-diff (codegen: regen byte-equality) В· smoke (web/service: liveness assert), (3) ABSORB the verified result into the domain SSOT вҖ” atlas embed В· *.bench.md В· .verdicts/<slug>/<id>.txt В· ledger, (4) feed the verified result as the next-frontier seed, (5) explicit disk checkpoint per round (throttle-resilient В· mining 0.5.0 discipline), (6) depletion test вҖ” 0 new leaf AND 0 new edge AND 0 new absorb AND all lenses @depleted = real-drained вҶ’ terminate; else ScheduleWakeup 1200s long interval (cache window). Guards inherited VERBATIM from mirror-loop вҖ” HONEST g63 (drill/verify SKIP/FALSIFIED/рҹҹ /рҹҹЎ/вҡӘ rejected, never absorbed; g5 paste-verbatim), IDEMPOTENT (adapter-appropriate dedup pre-check skips an already-absorbed result), ACTIVE-DOMAIN ONLY (g58 В· reads ~/.sidecar/active-domain В· refuse off-domain). Preset shorthands вҖ” `--adapter=atlas` вүҲ the old /mirror-loop (math atlas-fold loop) В· `--diverge-only` вүҲ the old /mining (discover stage only, no verify/absorb). Adapter EXTENSION hook вҖ” drop `~/.sidecar/loop-adapter/<name>.md` (verify-step + absorb-target schema) to add a custom adapter, mirroring mining's `~/.sidecar/lens/<name>.md`. ABSORBS + RETIRES /mining (now the discover sub-stage / --diverge-only) and /mirror-loop (now the --adapter=atlas preset). The /kick command is KEPT вҖ” it is the `hexa kick`/`hexa drill` CLI-verb wrapper that this discover stage CALLS, not an orchestrator. Defaults вҖ” adapter=auto В· engine=mk9 В· max-rounds=0 (NO cap В· run to genuine depletion, the default; explicit N>0 = optional backstop) В· budget=0 (local). plugins.name: hexa-goal
plugins.description: /hexa-goal [<goal>] [--adapter=atlas|bench|byte-diff|smoke] [--engine mk9|mk10] [--max-rounds N] вҖ” the CONVERGENT sister of /hexa-loop. Where hexa-loop drains an active domain to DEPLETION (divergent вҖ” open-ended lens divergence), hexa-goal CLIMBS toward a verifiable GOAL PREDICATE and terminates the instant the predicate is TRUE (convergent вҖ” goal-directed best-first). The verify-adapter mechanism + every guard are reused VERBATIM from hexa-loop; only TWO things change вҖ” (a) the DISCOVER stage is GAP-ANALYSIS BEST-FIRST (compute the goalвҲ’current gap, then select the SINGLE move that shrinks the gap the most) instead of lens divergence, and (b) the TERMINATION is GOAL-MET (the goal predicate evaluates TRUE) instead of depletion. The goal predicate reuses sbs-style `assert:` contracts (grep <pat> В· file <path> В· verdict <slug>/<id>); the default predicate (bare invocation) = ALL of the active domain's `- [ ]` milestones are checked. Each round: (1) GAP-ANALYSIS вҖ” measure goalвҲ’current, rank candidate moves by gap-reduction, pick the best-first single move, (2) рҹ”Ң VERIFY-ADAPTER auto-selected by active-domain kind вҖ” atlas (math: `hexa atlas register --from-drill` / `hexa verify`) В· bench (perf: byte-diff IDENTICAL + roofline % + О” vs baseline) В· byte-diff (codegen: regen byte-equality) В· smoke (web/service: liveness assert), (3) ABSORB the verified result into the domain SSOT вҖ” atlas embed В· *.bench.md В· .verdicts/<slug>/<id>.txt В· ledger, (4) RE-MEASURE progress vs the goal predicate, (5) explicit disk checkpoint per round, (6) GOAL-MET test вҶ’ predicate TRUE вҶ’ рҹҸҒ goal reached, terminate; max-rounds hit вҶ’ рҹ”„ cap reached, goal NOT met; else вҸ° + ScheduleWakeup 1200s. Guards inherited VERBATIM from hexa-loop вҖ” HONEST g63 (SKIP/FALSIFIED/рҹҹ /рҹҹЎ/вҡӘ rejected, never absorbed), IDEMPOTENT (dedup pre-check skips an already-absorbed result), ACTIVE-DOMAIN ONLY (g58 В· reads ~/.sidecar/active-domain В· refuse off-domain). Adapter EXTENSION hook вҖ” drop ~/.sidecar/loop-adapter/<name>.md to add a custom adapter, identical to hexa-loop. Defaults вҖ” adapter=auto В· engine=mk9 В· max-rounds=25 (a goal-seeking loop carries a finite cap so a never-met predicate cannot spin forever). Triggers вҖ” '/hexa-goal', 'н—ҘмӮ¬ кіЁ', 'лӘ©н‘ң лЈЁн”„', 'goal-directed loop', 'мҲҳл ҙ лЈЁн”„', 'goal-seeking loop', 'лӘ©н‘ң лҸ„лӢ¬ лЈЁн”„', 'climb to goal', 'converge to goal', 'gap-analysis loop'.

plugins.name: walkie
plugins.description: рҹ“» /walkie вҖ” session-to-session walkie-talkie over a UNIX domain socket, NO MCP (sidecar @D s15). Two Claude Code TUI sessions exchange one-line JSON messages: the CALL side is a CLI one-liner (`socat - UNIX-CONNECT:<peer>.sock`, fallback `nc -U <path>`); the receive side is the agent arming a persistent Monitor вҖ” `on` directly on `socat UNIX-LISTEN:<self>.sock,fork -`, OR, when the `walkie-arm` hook's capture daemon owns the socket and durably captures to `<self>.inbox.log`, on `tail -f <self>.inbox.log` (phase-2 coexistence вҖ” `on` auto-retargets) вҖ” so each received line surfaces as a chat task-notification. Local mailbox + roster under `~/.sidecar/walkie/` (roster.json maps handle -> socket path + sid + ts) so pool-route keeps it LOCAL. Radio verbs вҖ” `on` (listen: upsert self + print the Monitor command, retargeting to the inbox log when walkie-arm is present) В· `call <handle> <text...>` (direct; roster-miss falls back to the default `<handle>.sock` path so a peer that armed via `arm` вҖ” bind-only, unregistered вҖ” is still reachable, graceful peer-offline hint when not live) В· `all-call <text...>` (every peer) В· `scan` (roster table, alive marked via `test -S`, dead rows flagged not deleted) В· `handle <name>` В· `arm [<handle>]` (print the exact persistent-Monitor listen command for self or a named peer so a parent can paste it into a sub-agent prompt вҖ” main->sub steer) В· bare `/walkie` (status). OLD verbs (listen/send/broadcast/who/nick) still dispatch as silent aliases. NL triggers via the skill вҖ” 'л¬ҙм „' В· 'мӣҢнӮӨнҶ нӮӨ' В· 'м„ём…ҳлҒјлҰ¬ нҶөмӢ ' В· 'мҳҶ м„ём…ҳм—җ ліҙлӮҙ' В· 'call session' В· 'socket message' В· 'inter-session' В· 'steer sub-agent'.

plugins.name: walkie-arm
plugins.description: рҹ“» SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit hook вҖ” phase-2 AUTO-RECEIVE for the `walkie` plugin, NO MCP (sidecar @D s15). A UNIX socket is a connection not a queue, so phase-1 `/walkie on` drops every message that arrives while no one is bound. walkie-arm closes the gap: on SessionStart it upserts self into `~/.sidecar/walkie/roster.json` and spawns a DETACHED capture daemon (`socat UNIX-LISTEN:<self>.sock,fork OPEN:<self>.inbox.log,append,create`, fallback `nc -lkU`; setsidвҶ’nohup) that OWNS the socket and appends every received line to `<self>.inbox.log` вҖ” idempotent via a `<self>.daemon.pid` pidfile + pgrep guard, stale socket `rm -f` before bind. On SessionStart + every UserPromptSubmit it drains `<self>.inbox.log` from the byte offset in `<self>.offset`, emits new lines as additionalContext (`рҹ“Ё from X: ...`, capped ~20 with an overflow summary), and advances the offset (resets to 0 on truncation/rotation). Self handle from `~/.sidecar/walkie/self` (shared SSOT with the `walkie` command plugin). NO opt-out switch (s11) вҖ” gates only on real preconditions (handle set + socat|nc present), else graceful exit 0 (never blocks session start). hexa-lang (`_walkie_arm.hexa`). Pairs with `walkie` 0.3.0 whose `on` retargets to `tail -f <self>.inbox.log` when the daemon owns the socket.

plugins.name: walkie-mcp
plugins.description: рҹ“» walkie phase-3 IDLE-PUSH вҖ” an HTTP/SSE-transport MCP channels server (sidecar @D s15 1.8 allows HTTP/SSE MCP; STDIO MCP stays forbidden). walkie(0.3.0) + walkie-arm(0.1.1) capture inbound session-to-session lines into `~/.sidecar/walkie/<self>.inbox.log`, but an idle session only drains them on its NEXT turn (poll). This plugin closes that gap: a single local Node broker (Streamable HTTP on 127.0.0.1:7717) tails `<self>.inbox.log` from a dedicated `<self>.mcp.offset` (separate from walkie-arm's `.offset` so the two never contend) and, per new JSON line `{from,text,ts}`, emits an MCP channel notification `method:notifications/claude/channel` `params:{content:"рҹ“Ё from <handle>: <text>"}` вҖ” which Claude Code PUSHES into the live-but-idle session (no turn needed). The server declares the `experimental["claude/channel"]` capability the host requires; plugin.json exposes it as a `channels` entry + an http url-based `mcpServers` entry (NOT stdio command-based вҶ’ lint passes). receive-PUSH only вҖ” send stays the existing `/walkie call` socket path (no re-implementation). SessionStart fires a detached daemon-launcher (`_walkie_mcp_up.hexa`; pidfile + pgrep guard, walkie-arm pattern; on first run `npm install --omit=dev @modelcontextprotocol/sdk` into the plugin dir if node_modules absent вҖ” node_modules never committed). sidecar's FIRST non-hexa (Node) executable. IDLE-PUSH requires Claude Code launched with `--dangerously-load-development-channels walkie-mcp` (or `--channels walkie-mcp` once GA) AND a first-party provider (Bedrock/Vertex skip) вҖ” experimental research-preview. NO opt-out switch (s11) вҖ” gates only on real preconditions (node present В· handle set), else graceful exit 0. NO absolute paths ($HOME/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only). 0.1.2 вҖ” STATEFUL multi-session transport fix: per-session Server+transport keyed by the SDK session id (was a single shared Server re-connect()ed per request, which orphaned a prior client's long-lived push SSE stream and surfaced host-side as 'socket connection closed unexpectedly' mid-turn); channel emits now fan out to EVERY connected session.

plugins.name: walkie-hexa
plugins.description: рҹ“Ў SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit hook вҖ” the EMIT/sender side of the walkie family for hexa-lang releases (NO MCP, sidecar @D s15). Watches `$HOME/core/hexa-lang` git HEAD: on every SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit it reads `git -C $HOME/core/hexa-lang rev-parse --short HEAD` and, when the sha DIFFERS from the last-broadcast one in `~/.sidecar/walkie/hexa-lang.last-sha`, auto-broadcasts a one-line `рҹ“Ў hexa-lang <short-sha>: <commit subject>` to EVERY live peer via the walkie all-call roster loop (verbatim from `/walkie all-call` вҖ” resolve each handle's sock from `~/.sidecar/walkie/roster.json`, build a `{from,text,ts}` JSON line, send via `socat - UNIX-CONNECT:<sock>` else `nc -U <sock>`, skip self, skip offline peers). Receiving is ALREADY handled by walkie-arm (capture daemon) + walkie-mcp (idle-push) вҖ” this plugin ONLY emits, never re-implements receive. Per-sha atomic `mkdir ~/.sidecar/walkie/.hexa-lang-lock-<sha>` lock вҶ’ exactly ONE session fires per new sha even under concurrent ticks (the loser just records the sha). FIRST-SEEN (no last-sha file) records the current sha SILENTLY (not an update). Missing repo (`test -e <repo>/.git`) / unset self handle (`~/.sidecar/walkie/self`) / git failure вҶ’ graceful exit 0. Never reads stdin (hexa fake-stdin trap вҖ” env + files + exec only; no self-path re-spawn). NO opt-out switch (s11) вҖ” gates only on real preconditions (hexa-lang/.git present + self handle set), else silent exit 0. hexa-lang (`_walkie_hexa.hexa tick`, via `hexa run`). NO absolute paths ($HOME/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only).

plugins.name: celebrate
plugins.description: рҹҺҶ Stop hook that fires a fireworks celebration when the ACTIVE /domain's progress crosses to 100% (a 'novel goal' reached). On every turn-end it resolves the active domain (same convention as the `domain` skill вҖ” git repo root + `$HOME/.claude/domain-active.tsv` active pointer, else project basename; snapshot path via the repo's `DOMAINS.tape` roster) and counts `- [ ]`/`- [x]` milestones in the active DOMAIN.md (done/total; 100% = total>0 && done==total). It keeps the last-seen percent per domain in `$HOME/.sidecar/celebrate/<domain-key>.seen` and fires ONLY on the crossing (prev <100 вҶ’ now =100), then records 100 so it never re-fires while it stays at 100; if it drops below 100 and re-reaches, it celebrates again (natural). On the crossing the hook (1) emits a multi-line ASCII fireworks burst + 'рҹҺҶ <DOMAIN> @goal 100% лӢ¬м„ұ!' via the top-level `systemMessage` field into the chat (0.1.2 вҖ” Stop hooks do NOT support hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext), (2) rings the terminal bell (`printf '\a'`). HARD CONSTRAINT: the Claude Code TUI canvas is owned by Ink, so this hook NEVER writes ANSI/animation to /dev/tty (that would corrupt the screen) вҖ” the animation lives ONLY in the spawned separate window; the in-stream ASCII burst is static text. Non-macOS вҶ’ skips (3), keeps (1)(2). UNCONDITIONAL (commons @D s11 вҖ” no env-var/flag/config escape hatch): the ONLY gates are real preconditions вҖ” an active domain exists AND has вүҘ1 milestone AND progress just crossed 100; no active domain / 0 milestones / not crossing вҶ’ silent exit 0. hexa-lang (`_celebrate.hexa` + `_fireworks.hexa`, via `hexa run`); reads stdin via `exec("cat")` (avoids the hexa fake-FILE segfault), checks path existence via shell `test -e`, portable `$HOME`/`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths only.
